### 1.1 Revision lineage and closure rule

Revision 12 is the last issued historical Contract 1 Candidate Lock. Revisions 13–17 are preserved immutable historical drafts and are not current upstream authorities. Revision 18 is the last fully reviewed, locked (but not repository-persisted) candidate, and is the direct upstream baseline for this Revision 19.

Revision 19 is a scope-extension successor to Revision 18. It makes the following normative additions and corrections, all recorded in full in Annex AF (Revision 19 Targeted Extension Record):

```text
1. 11 new canonical Layer 1 Canonical Space Type rows (Annex S), all
   Owner-selected and transitioned directly to active_candidate: the 10
   standalone additions dining_room, home_office, library, pantry,
   laundry_room, utility_room, mechanical_room, basement, veranda and
   winter_garden, plus the stable canonical room identity
   space.residential.kitchen_living_room.
2. 3 new Space Subtype entries (Annex S1): children_room,
   guest_bedroom, primary_bedroom — parentCanonicalSpaceTypeId =
   space.residential.bedroom.
3. 1 new auxiliary Composite Space Profile metadata record (new Annex
   AG): composite.residential.kitchen_living_room, which describes the
   canonical room identity space.residential.kitchen_living_room and
   records components space.residential.living_room +
   space.residential.kitchen. The metadata record is not a 35th room
   category and is never used as Room.spaceType.
4. 4 new Space Families (Annex R): family.residential.dining,
   family.residential.work_study, family.residential.utility,
   family.residential.below_grade. These supporting taxonomy identities
   remain inactive and are not additional active user-facing categories.
5. Layer 2/Profile Activation transition (Annex J.3 axis; Annex AA
   registry) for all 34 residential room categories and the separate
   Composite Space Profile metadata record to active_candidate.
   Supporting family identities remain inactive. active_locked is
   explicitly NOT assigned (Section 1 above).
6. Facility-to-Space applicability rows (Annex V) for all 11 new
   canonical room types: 48 rows for the 10 standalone additions plus
   5 rows for space.residential.kitchen_living_room, 53 rows total.
7. Localization coverage for the 14 new user-facing room-category
   identities (10 standalone canonical additions + 3 bedroom
   specializations + kitchen_living_room) in EN and RU. Annex U1 also
   carries one separate EN label for the auxiliary Composite Space
   Profile metadata record, yielding 15 new Annex U1 rows. Existing KK
   values are preserved and KK coverage is not expanded.
8. Resolution of the Annex AB.1 unresolved_legacy_runtime_value gap
   for dining_room and home_office. The generic legacy literals
   `library` and `terrace` remain explicitly unbound because the
   repository snapshot does not verify an exact Residential-34
   semantic target for either literal; cross-domain guessing is
   prohibited.
9. Track E compatibility identity hooks (new Annex AH) — 17 governed
   hook rows: 3 current bounded-operation identities and 14 reserved,
   non-activating future-compatibility references covering PropertyId,
   ProjectId, SessionId, RoomView/RoomState versioning, inter-room
   relations, project memory, cross-session provenance and future
   spatial-source references.
10. Sections 2, 2a, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12 updated to reflect the
    34-category scope; Annex AD (Historical Drafting Lineage)
    extended with a Revision 19 entry.
```

Revision 19 preserves without semantic change everything Revision 18
preserved from Revision 17 (Section 1.1 historical text above,
unchanged), plus every Revision 18 provision not named in the list
above: all 16 Platform Domains; all 195 Layer 1 Canonical Space Types
prior to this revision's 11 additions (now 206); the exact prior
Owner-selected Layer 2 inventory prior to this revision's activation
transition; the full Facility-to-Space matrix prior to this
revision's 11-type extension; all Shared Concept Groups, runtime
bindings, sanitary definitions (Annex Z.2 — unchanged, already
resolved as the authoritative four-category model), schemas,
governance boundaries and non-authorization rules from Revision 18.

Any later content change requires a new revision and hash. Final
consolidated review is a separate action against the exact unchanged
Revision 19 artifact.

## 2. Purpose, platform foundation and bounded activation

### 2.1 Purpose

Contract 1 owns VistaRoom AI's provider-independent semantic vocabulary, identity boundaries, normalization, lifecycle and the full-platform space foundation. It supports perception, reasoning, explainability, controlled editing, catalog intelligence and future whole-project memory.

### 2.2 Three normative layers

```text
Layer 1 — full-platform Master Vocabulary and Space Foundation:
16 Platform Domains and 206 Canonical Space Types (195 from Revision
18, plus 11 new residential types added by Revision 19 — the 10
standalone canonical additions plus space.residential.kitchen_living_room,
which is itself a normal canonical Space Type row, distinct from its
Annex AG composite metadata record).

Layer 2 Operational — accepted current bounded profile:
1-6 governed ImageAsset objects per RoomCase, one physical room, one
operation, 34 residential room categories (Candidate A Bounded Scope
Decision Rev5, in-place corrected, commit
565a3a03294086f319ccec5ff2e77afb5af8a9e1) — replacing the historical
"one image, one room, one operation, five residential room
identities" profile.

Layer 2 Candidate — Owner-selected profile:
304 identities (285 from Revision 18, plus 19 added by Revision 19),
including 143 Canonical Space Types across 10 domains. Within the
residential domain, all 34 room categories (`kitchen_living_room`
included among them, not a 35th) are owner_selected_effective /
active_candidate, and the separate Composite Space Profile metadata
record describing `kitchen_living_room` (Annex AG) is likewise
active_candidate (Annex S, Annex AA, Annex J.3) — replacing the
historical blanket
owner_selected/not_yet_effective status that applied to all Layer 2
Candidate identities in Revision 18. active_locked is not assigned to
any identity by this revision (Section 1; Annex J.3).
```

Layer 2 Candidate references Layer 1 identities and never duplicates their definitions.

### 2.3 Current bounded activation — 34-category residential operational profile (Revision 19)

```text
living_room                bedroom                     children_room
guest_bedroom              primary_bedroom             kitchen
dining_room                kitchen_living_room (canonical room identity; related Composite Space Profile metadata in Annex AG)
home_office                library                     bathroom
toilet_room                shower_room                 combined_bathroom
entryway                   vestibule                   hall
corridor                   dressing_room               walk_in_closet
pantry                     laundry_room                utility_room
mechanical_room            staircase_space             stair_hall
attic                      mansard_room                basement
garage                     balcony                     terrace
veranda                    winter_garden
```

All 34 categories — including `kitchen_living_room`, not a 35th — are active_candidate as of this Revision 19 (Annex AA); the separate Composite Space Profile metadata record describing `kitchen_living_room` (Annex AG) is likewise active_candidate, replacing the historical five-category (`living_room`, `bedroom`, `kitchen`, `bathroom`, `toilet_room`) operational profile referenced by Revision 18. `active_locked` — full, final activation — is not conferred by this draft; `active_locked` and root-transition effectiveness require explicit Project Owner acceptance of the complete Atomic Root-Transition Package (Bounded Scope + Contract 1 Revision 19 + Evaluation Threshold Plan successor + Test Data Handling successor + Module Applicability Profile successor; Section 1; Annex J.3).

### 2.4 Contract 1 ownership

Contract 1 owns the Layer 1 registries, the cross-layer applicability/binding registries, and governance of Layer 2 Candidate membership, wave assignment and profile activation status. Evaluation methodology, corpus, provider/model evaluation, implementation and production remain downstream-owned and unauthorized.

### 2.5 Room/space activation ownership — prepared supersession of Preparation Plan Rev11 Decision R11.3

Preparation Plan Rev11 (Accepted, Project Owner, 2026-07-18 — not a draft, despite its own internal "remains a draft" self-description, per the independently verified external Owner Acceptance record) remains governing until atomic root-transition acceptance. This Revision 19 is itself part of that atomic root-transition package (Root Impact Analysis and Work Plan, Phase 4). Upon that atomic acceptance — not conferred by this draft alone — its statement that Contract 1 does not own room-type activation is superseded by this model:

```text
Contract 1 owns Layer 1 identity and governs Layer 2 Candidate membership/status.
Downstream documents govern evaluation, corpus, implementation and production authorization.
```

No period of dual effective authority is created.

## 2a. Normative topology

### 2a.1 Facility taxonomy

```text
Platform Domain
└── Facility Context
```

### 2a.2 Space taxonomy

```text
Platform Domain
└── Space Family
    └── Canonical Space Type
        └── Space Subtype
```

### 2a.3 Relation registries

```text
Facility Context ↔ Facility-to-Space Applicability ↔ Canonical Space Type
Canonical Space Type ↔ Space-to-Zone Applicability ↔ Functional Zone
Canonical Space Type ↔ Space Use Applicability ↔ Space Use
```

### 2a.4 Orthogonal axes

Property Composition, Environment/Enclosure, Attachment Mode, Lifecycle/Migration, Activation Role, Evidence Mode and Evaluation Applicability are not hierarchy levels.

### 2a.5 Required Canonical Space Type record schema

Every one of the 206 Layer 1 Canonical Space Types in Annex S contains (11 of the 206 — the residential types added by Revision 19, including `space.residential.kitchen_living_room` — were created directly with this complete record schema, not retrofitted):

```text
canonicalSpaceTypeId
platformDomainId
parentSpaceFamilyId
sharedConceptGroupId
normativeDefinition
positiveEvidence
negativeEvidence
excludes
requiredOrTypicalFeatures
applicableFacilityContexts
environmentAndEnclosure
attachmentModeApplicability
allowedFunctionalZones
allowedSpaceUses
ambiguityFallback
runtimeBindingKind
runtimeBindingStatus
lifecycleStatus
layer2SelectionStatus
```

Annex S is the sole normative Canonical Space Type Registry. Annex G contains no normative Canonical Space Type rows. All environment and attachment values resolve to Annex P tokens.

Unknown branch:
no value field

outside Contract 1 type-label vocabulary

### 3.2 SpaceType alignment facts

Current runtime values are imported without semantic guessing. Exact mappings and explicit no-exact-target outcomes are owned by Annex AB. `toilet_room` remains semantically resolved but runtime emission is not authorized.

### 3.3 RegistryVersionSet

`RegistryVersionSet` is the Contract-1-owned version map recorded by each normalization operation for every registry actually consulted by that operation. The table below is the current root version index for the registries introduced, changed, or directly governing the Revision 19 Residential-34 transition; auxiliary and historical registries retain the row-level version tokens declared in their own normative sources and are added to an operation's `sourceRegistryVersions` whenever consulted. The logical Master Vocabulary has disjoint physical partitions: Annex G owns non-space Master concepts; Annex S owns Canonical Space Types; Annex S0 owns Components. Each identity has exactly one normative owner.

| Registry ID | Version / commit | Sole normative source | Scope |
|---|---|---|---|
| masterEntityVocabulary | mv.entity.candidate-a.1.0.0-candidate | Annex G | non-space Master concepts |
| canonicalSpaceType | mv.space.candidate-a.4.0.0-candidate | Annex S | 206 Canonical Space Types (195 from Revision 18, plus 11 new residential types, including space.residential.kitchen_living_room) |
| componentIdentity | mv.component.candidate-a.1.0.0-candidate | Annex S0 | 4 stair components |
| spaceSubtype | mv.subtype.candidate-a.1.0.0-candidate | Annex S1 | 3 Space Subtypes (new registry population, Revision 19) |
| compositeSpaceProfile | mv.composite.candidate-a.1.0.0-candidate | Annex AG | 1 Composite Space Profile (new registry, Revision 19) |
| preprocessingProfile | pp.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate | §4.6 | normalization-only successor profile: Unicode NFC, whitespace normalization and locale-independent case folding; lookup is node-class-specific and external to preprocessing |
| bindingRegistry | br.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate | Annexes A1–A5 | directed profile, wire, context-selection and diagnostic-collapse bindings; successor adds complete Residential-34 sink context coverage |
| namespaceStatus | ns.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate | Annex J.6 | governed populated, reserved and deprecated-alias namespace patterns; successor adds material, finish, color and color-space reservations |
| recordSchema | rs.candidate-a.4.0.0-candidate | Annex L | normative record and closed-value schemas through Revision 19 |
| activeProfile | vp.candidate-a.five-room.1.0.0-candidate | Annex B | historical object/structural-element evaluation profile — **not updated by Revision 19** (Annex B note above); the version literal retains its historical "five-room" name as a stable, immutable historical identifier string for that specific object-profile registry, and must never be read as a current statement about room-category count |
| residentialRoomProfile | sp.candidate-a.residential-34.1.0.0-candidate | Annex A0.2 (all 34 room bindings), Annex A0.3 (RU profile localization for all 34), Annex U2 (all Residential-34 EN/RU room aliases, aggregated/referenced here, not duplicated) | current Residential-34 room profile — governs all 34 current room-category binding rows (Annex A0.2), all 34 active room localization rows (Annex A0.3), and all current EN/RU room-alias rows (Annex U2, the sole normative alias owner), subtype handling (children_room/guest_bedroom/primary_bedroom), composite handling (kitchen_living_room), current room activation status, and current room downstream identity mapping, in full, for every one of the 34 active residential categories. The historical `sp.candidate-a.five-room.1.0.0-candidate` identifier survives only as explicit historical migration provenance text within Annex A0.2's evidence-snapshot column (rows `c1.space.001`–`c1.space.005`) and remains the current version for unrelated historical object/structural registries (Annex B; Annex D's Object/StructuralElement rows and its preserved historical five-room room-alias subset); it is not the current row version for any of the 34 room-category rows in Annex A0.2, Annex A0.3, or Annex U2. Annex D is not converted into, and does not duplicate, the Residential-34 room-alias registry — Annex U2 is its sole normative owner. |
| layer2Candidate | l2.candidate-a.rev2-candidate | Annex AA | 304 identities (285 from Revision 18, plus 19 new); the 34 residential room categories (`kitchen_living_room` included, not a 35th) are `active_candidate`, and the separate Composite Space Profile metadata record is likewise `active_candidate` — not uniformly `not-yet-effective`/`inactive` as under Revision 18 (version bumped rev1→rev2 by Revision 19 — the row content, not merely the count, changed: effectiveActivationStatus enum values were corrected, Section 4.2) |
| applicability | app.candidate-a.rev2-candidate | Annexes V–X | exact relation registries (version bumped rev1→rev2 by Revision 19 — Annex V gained 53 new rows) |
| aliasLocalization | al.candidate-a.rev2-candidate | Annex U1 (EN labels for Facility Context, Canonical Space Type, Space Subtype and Composite Space Profile identities) and Annex U2 (full-platform canonical aliases, including all Residential-34 room aliases) | current full-platform labels and aliases (version bumped rev1→rev2 by Revision 19 — Annex U1 gained 15 new rows; Annex U2 gained 52 new rows, alias.full.0282-0333, including EN/RU room aliases and exact punctuation-preserving aliases required by the fixed preprocessing profile; Annex A0.3 is separately versioned by `sp.candidate-a.residential-34.1.0.0-candidate`) |
| legacyActiveAlias | ar.candidate-a.1.0.0-candidate | Annexes D–E | immutable historical Object/StructuralElement alias and contextual-alias paths, including the preserved five-room room-alias subset; not the Residential-34 room-alias owner |
| failureCode | fc.candidate-a.1.0.0-candidate | Annex I.1 | Contract-1 normalization and candidate-lock failure codes |
| externalAlignmentRequirementCode | arc.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate | Annex I.2 | runtime-alignment requirement codes for every Residential-34 binding whose target external SpaceType token is not present in the imported runtime registry |
| runtimeBinding | rb.candidate-a.rev2-candidate | Annex AB | exact runtime bindings (version bumped rev1→rev2 by Revision 19 — `dining_room` and `home_office` became exact canonical bindings; generic `library` and `terrace` remain explicit no-exact-target legacy values) |
| crossCuttingCompatibility | cc.candidate-a.1.0.0-candidate | Annex AI | five cross-cutting compatibility-only foundation hook records (new registry, Phase 4 final narrow closure pass) |
| trackECompatibility | tE.candidate-a.1.0.0-candidate | Annex AH | reserved, non-activating Track E identity hooks (new registry, Revision 19) |
| repositorySnapshot | ea2607a348d9be2259d46945e09fd010d6777969 | repository | imported code facts |

## 4. Owned semantic model

### 4.1 Identity layers

```text
MasterConceptId
MasterBoundaryRuleId
ActiveProfileConceptId
SpaceProfileConceptId
RoomCategoryIdentityId
StructuredScene wire literal
SpaceTypeId
TargetExternalSpaceTypeId
CanonicalSpaceTypeId
SpaceSubtypeId
External label
InputLabelSource
RegistryVersionSet
```

No layer silently substitutes for another.

`RoomCategoryIdentityId` is the Contract-1-owned closed union for
semantic room-category identity:

```text
RoomCategoryIdentityId =
    CanonicalSpaceTypeId
  | SpaceSubtypeId
```

A `RoomCategoryIdentityId` is always a fully qualified stable identity
owned by Annex S or Annex S1. It is never a wire literal or runtime
`SpaceTypeId`. The three bedroom specializations therefore retain their
own subtype identities and never collapse into
`space.residential.bedroom`.

`TargetExternalSpaceTypeId` is a Contract-1-owned proposed serialization/alignment token. It may equal an existing imported runtime `SpaceTypeId`, or it may name a required future registry extension. A value that is absent from the imported runtime enum is therefore a `TargetExternalSpaceTypeId`, never a current `SpaceTypeId`. Only `emittedSpaceTypeId` carries an actual authorized runtime `SpaceTypeId`.


### 4.2 Independent state axes

```text
masterLifecycleStatus:
reserved | experimental | active | deprecated | retired

profileActivationStatus:
inactive | active_candidate | active_locked | profile_deprecated

Layer2SelectionStatus:
owner_selected_not_yet_effective | owner_selected_effective | layer1_defined_not_selected

profileSelectionStatus:
owner_selected

effectiveActivationStatus:
inactive | active_candidate | active_locked | profile_deprecated
(the same closed enum as profileActivationStatus above — Annex AA's
effectiveActivationStatus column is the per-identity, per-row
instantiation of this one axis, not a second, independent axis. As of
Revision 19, no duplicate axis is retained: the historical single
fixed literal not_yet_effective is superseded by inactive as the
starting value of this same enum, and Annex AA rows read
active_candidate for every currently transitioned identity.)

schemaRepresentability:
schema_compatible_open_string | governed_space_type_reference

implementationReadiness:
not_implemented | implemented

runtimeEmissionAuthorization:
not_authorized | authorized

externalSpaceTypeAlignmentStatus:
existing_verified | registry_extension_required

localizationStatus:
localized | not_yet_localized | not_applicable

localizationEntryLifecycleStatus:
active | deprecated

aliasEntryLifecycleStatus:
active | deprecated
```

`Layer2SelectionStatus` is a closed Contract-1-owned convenience status used by global Layer 1 registries:

```text
owner_selected_not_yet_effective:
the identity is present in Annex AA with profileSelectionStatus = owner_selected and effectiveActivationStatus = inactive (historical Revision 18 identities not yet transitioned).

owner_selected_effective:
the identity is present in Annex AA with profileSelectionStatus = owner_selected and effectiveActivationStatus = active_candidate (all 34 residential room categories, `kitchen_living_room` included among them and not a 35th, plus the separate Composite Space Profile metadata record describing it, Annex AG — as of Revision 19).

layer1_defined_not_selected:
the identity is defined in Layer 1 and has no Layer 2 Candidate membership or activation row.
```

The convenience value never replaces Annex AA's independent selection and effectiveness fields. Master lifecycle, profile activation, Layer 2 selection and effective activation remain independent axes; `effectiveActivationStatus` and `profileActivationStatus` are the same axis (one definition, applied at two levels — general and per-row), not two independent axes, as of this Revision 19 clarification.

### 4.3 Directed binding types

```text
SpaceProfileBinding
MasterToProfileBinding
ProfileToWireBinding
WireToProfileResolution
ContextSelectionMember
ContextSelectionSet
DiagnosticCollapseBinding
```

### 4.4 Semantic identity exported downstream

For StructuralElement/Object, Contract 1 exports
`ActiveProfileConceptId`. For Room, it exports
`SpaceProfileConceptId` and its exact `RoomCategoryIdentityId`. That
identity may be an Annex S `CanonicalSpaceTypeId` or an Annex S1
`SpaceSubtypeId`; specializations remain independent semantic results.
The target wire literal or runtime `SpaceTypeId` is an independently
controlled serialization/emission value and never substitutes for
semantic room identity.

### 4.5 InputLabelSource × node-class execution matrix

| Source node class | InputLabelSource | Preprocessing | Resolution path | Allowed terminal outcomes | Emission field |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Room | trusted_internal_wire | none | exact current/canonical SpaceType identity → SpaceProfileBinding | resolved, invalid, unsupported_version | emittedSpaceTypeId only when alignment exists and runtime emission is authorized |
| Room | external_provider_text | pp.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate | space Alias → Master space → SpaceProfileBinding | resolved, unknown_due_to_evidence, unresolved, unsupported_version | emittedSpaceTypeId under the same authorization rule |
| Room | annotation_text | pp.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate | space Alias → Master space → SpaceProfileBinding | resolved, unknown_due_to_evidence, unresolved, unsupported_version | emittedSpaceTypeId under the same authorization rule |
| Room | user_text | pp.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate | space Alias → Master space → SpaceProfileBinding | resolved, unknown_due_to_evidence, unresolved | emittedSpaceTypeId under the same authorization rule |
| Room | legacy_artifact | recorded versions | direct historical binding or explicit versioned adapter | resolved, invalid, unsupported_version | emittedSpaceTypeId only when current emission is separately authorized |
| StructuralElement/Object | trusted_internal_wire | none | WireToProfileResolution → MasterToProfileBinding inverse identity | resolved, invalid, unsupported_version | emittedTypeLabelLiteral only when runtime emission is authorized |
| StructuralElement/Object | external_provider_text | pp.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate | Alias/ContextualAlias → Master → disambiguation/context → Profile → wire | resolved, unresolved, unknown_due_to_evidence, known_but_unbound, invalid, unsupported_version | emittedTypeLabelLiteral under the authorization rule |
| StructuralElement/Object | annotation_text | pp.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate | Alias/ContextualAlias → Master → disambiguation/context → Profile → wire | resolved, unresolved, unknown_due_to_evidence, known_but_unbound, invalid, unsupported_version | emittedTypeLabelLiteral under the authorization rule |
| StructuralElement/Object | user_text | pp.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate | Alias/ContextualAlias → Master → disambiguation/context → Profile → wire | resolved, unresolved, unknown_due_to_evidence, known_but_unbound, invalid | emittedTypeLabelLiteral under the authorization rule |
| StructuralElement/Object | legacy_artifact | recorded versions | direct historical binding or explicit versioned adapter | resolved, invalid, unsupported_version | emittedTypeLabelLiteral only when current emission is separately authorized |

### 4.6 Fixed preprocessing profile and normalization paths

For textual sources, `normalizedText` is produced by exactly this fixed,
versioned profile:

```text
preprocessingProfileId:
pp.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate

successorOf:
pp.candidate-a.1.0.0-candidate

operations in order:
1. Unicode NFC normalization
2. trim leading/trailing Unicode whitespace
3. collapse internal Unicode whitespace runs to one U+0020
4. locale-independent Unicode default case folding
```

The preprocessing profile produces `normalizedText` only. Registry
lookup is a separate, node-class-specific resolution step and is not an
operation inside `pp.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate`.

No stemming, fuzzy matching, embeddings, transliteration, punctuation
removal, spelling correction or undeclared transformation is permitted.
This fixed profile is a narrow contract boundary, not a general
production Unicode/locale-processing subsystem.

**Room wire path:** exact input is matched first to
`currentExternalSpaceTypeId`, then to `targetExternalSpaceTypeId`.
Existing runtime IDs and `toilet_room` all resolve semantically through
Annex A0.2. Runtime absence is recorded by alignment status/code, not by
changing semantic `mappingStatus`.

**Room text path:** preprocessing profile → Annex U2 exact
room-category alias lookup using the composite key
`locale + normalizedText + platformDomainScope = platform.residential +
identityKind ∈ {canonical_space_type, space_subtype}` → Master room
identity → Annex A0.2 SpaceProfileBinding → exact Residential-34 room
identity. Alias uniqueness is required within this complete composite
key; the same normalized text may validly exist in another platform
domain. Annex D/E remain the StructuralElement/Object text path (below),
not the room text path.

**StructuralElement/Object trusted-wire path:** exact node-class/wire
lookup in Annex A3.

**StructuralElement/Object text path:** Annex D/E → Annex F where
required → Master identity → Annex A1/A4/A5 → Annex A2.

### 4.7 Fallback

```text
exact active binding
→ registered diagnostic collapse when authorized
→ unknown_due_to_evidence
→ known_but_unbound with preserved Master identity
```

Unknown carries no value. Atypical room placement never invalidates a
visually confirmed Master concept.

### 4.8 Registry source-of-truth rule

Each registry/value definition has exactly one sole normative source
listed in §3.3. Body sections may define execution semantics and schemas
may describe fields, but neither duplicates registry rows.

---

## 5. Normative rules and record/value schemas

### 5.1 Normative rules

1. Master Vocabulary remains full-platform and profile-independent.
2. Active Evaluation Profile is residential-only and covers exactly 34 room categories (kitchen_living_room included among the 34, not an additional 35th), with a governed 1–6 ImageAsset capture set per exactly one active RoomCase, one physical room, one materially consistent state (Candidate A Bounded Scope Decision Rev5, Section 6.0/Section 8A, in-place corrected). The historical "five-room bounded" rule is superseded; the literal token `five-room` survives only inside explicitly labelled immutable historical version identifiers (e.g. `vp.candidate-a.five-room.1.0.0-candidate`, Annex B), never as a current normative statement.
3. Stable IDs are never reused.
4. Master hierarchy is acyclic and parent-complete.
5. Open `Observed<string>` compatibility is preserved.
6. Unknown has no invented value.
7. External text resolves Master-first.
8. Trusted wire requires recorded profile/binding versions.
9. Every active node-class/wire pair has one profile owner.
10. Context unknown, context no-match and ambiguity are distinct outcomes.
11. Disambiguation never guesses and one record resolves one entity.
12. Diagnostic collapse is one-way and preserves Master identity.
13. Typical room placement is advisory.
14. Semantic resolution, external alignment and runtime emission are separate.
15. `toilet_room` is semantically resolved but non-emittable until alignment and authorization.
16. Contract 1 defines only its semantic/alignment code behavior.
17. Locked registries are immutable; change requires successor versions.
18. Contract 1 preserves sufficient version/migration metadata for backward-read path selection; external historical-resource absence may still yield `unsupported_version`.
19. Reserved namespaces create no implementation, corpus or evaluation duty.
20. Nothing in Contract 1 authorizes downstream drafting before candidate-lock issuance or implementation work.

### 5.2 Record Schema Registry reference

Annex L contains the normative schema for every record and value type introduced through Revision 19, including all Layer 1, Layer 2 (canonical, subtype, composite), applicability, activation, Track E hook, and runtime-binding records.

### 5.3 MasterVocabularyEntry

Annex G implements:

```text
masterConceptId
semanticParentId
conceptType
semanticFamily
canonicalLabelEN
localizedLabelsRef
identityBoundarySummary
inclusionRuleId
exclusionRuleId
installationApplicability
assemblyPolicy
masterLifecycleStatus
introducedVersion
deprecatedVersion
replacementId
```

### 5.4 NormalizationRecord

| Field | Type | Required | Nullable | Condition / invariant |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| recordId | NormalizationRecordId | yes | no | unique |
| inputLabelSource | enum | yes | no | one §4.5 value |
| sourceNodeClass | enum | yes | no | Room, StructuralElement or Object |
| rawLabel | string | conditional | yes | required for textual sources; preserved verbatim |
| rawLocale | LocaleId | conditional | yes | recorded when known |
| preprocessingProfileId | PreprocessingProfileId | conditional | yes | required for textual sources; exact §4.6 profile |
| sourceSpaceTypeId | SpaceTypeId/null | conditional | yes | Room trusted-wire/legacy path only |
| sourceRegistryVersions | RegistryVersionSet | yes | no | exact consulted RegistryId→VersionToken entries |
| candidateMasterConceptIds | StableId[] | yes | no | empty unless contextual/ambiguous candidates exist |
| masterConceptId | StableId/null | conditional | yes | present after Master resolution and for known_but_unbound |
| activeProfileConceptId | ActiveProfileConceptId/null | conditional | yes | StructuralElement/Object result |
| spaceProfileConceptId | SpaceProfileConceptId/null | conditional | yes | Room result |
| roomCategoryIdentityId | RoomCategoryIdentityId/null | conditional | yes | Room; required after semantic resolution; exact Annex S canonical or Annex S1 subtype identity |
| resolvedBindingIds | BindingId[] | yes | no | every applied binding recorded |
| disambiguationRuleId | RuleId/null | conditional | yes | required when Annex F invoked |
| disambiguationEvidenceRefs | EvidenceRef[] | yes | no | opaque downstream-owned references |
| mappingStatus | enum | yes | no | one terminal value below |
| emittedTypeLabelLiteral | string/null | conditional | yes | StructuralElement/Object; only when resolved and runtime authorized |
| targetExternalSpaceTypeId | TargetExternalSpaceTypeId/null | conditional | yes | Room serialization/alignment target only; may name an existing imported runtime value or a required future registry-extension token and never replaces roomCategoryIdentityId |
| emittedSpaceTypeId | SpaceTypeId/null | conditional | yes | Room; only when resolved, externally aligned and runtime authorized |
| externalSpaceTypeAlignmentStatus | enum/null | conditional | yes | Room only |
| externalAlignmentRequirementCode | ExternalAlignmentRequirementCode/null | conditional | yes | Room only; Annex I.2 |
| failureCode | FailureCode/null | conditional | yes | required for non-resolved terminal outcomes; Annex I.1 |
| auditTraceId | TraceId | yes | no | traceability without defining full diagnosability architecture |

Terminal `mappingStatus` enum:

```text
resolved
unresolved
unknown_due_to_evidence
known_but_unbound
invalid
unsupported_version
```

| Status | Meaning | Failure code |
|---|---|---|
| resolved | Master and Active/Space Profile identity are unambiguous; runtime emission may still be unavailable or unauthorized | none |
| unresolved | multiple candidates remain viable | required |
| unknown_due_to_evidence | evidence/label is insufficient to identify Master or room context | required |
| known_but_unbound | Master identity is known but no applicable Active Profile/context/collapse binding exists | required |
| invalid | source/record violates this Contract | required |
| unsupported_version | required historical/consulted version cannot be used | required |

`resolved` alone may carry a canonical target or eventual emission. An
external-alignment requirement does not populate `failureCode` and does
not change `resolved`.

### 5.5 One-record/one-entity rule

One `NormalizationRecord` resolves one independently observed room,
entity or component. Multi-entity provider text is split by the caller
into multiple records under its own batch/evidence envelope.

### 5.6 Lifecycle, profile activation and migration

Annex J is authoritative. Master lifecycle transitions use
`VocabularyChangeRecord`/`DeprecationRecord`. Profile activation uses a
separate `ProfileActivationRecord`. No record substitutes for another.

---

## 6. Non-normative execution examples

### Example 1 — trusted object wire

```text
Object + trusted_internal_wire + sofa
→ profile.candidate_a.sofa
→ entity.furniture.seating.sofa
→ mappingStatus: resolved
```

### Example 2 — provider text

```text
raw: Couch
preprocessed: couch
→ entity.furniture.seating.sofa
→ profile.candidate_a.sofa
→ mappingStatus: resolved
```

### Example 3 — unknown

Insufficient evidence produces `unknown_due_to_evidence` and no emitted
value.

### Example 4 — diagnostic collapse

`entity.opening.archway` remains the Master identity and may collapse to
`profile.candidate_a.opening`; the original Master ID is preserved.

### Example 5 — atypical sink placement

A visually confirmed sink in a living room remains
`entity.fixed_element.sanitary.sink` with `known_but_unbound`; no
kitchen/bathroom profile is guessed.

### Example 6 — toilet room before runtime alignment

```text
masterConceptId: space.residential.toilet_room
spaceProfileConceptId: profile.space.toilet_room
roomCategoryIdentityId: space.residential.toilet_room
mappingStatus: resolved
targetExternalSpaceTypeId: toilet_room
externalSpaceTypeAlignmentStatus: registry_extension_required
externalAlignmentRequirementCode: SPACE_TYPE_EXTERNAL_MISSING
emittedSpaceTypeId: null
```

### Example 7 — legacy artifact

The contract selects direct reading or a declared versioned adapter. If
a required external historical resource is unavailable, outcome is
`unsupported_version`; no current identity is silently substituted.

---

## 7. Validation and conformance rules

Revision 19 validation fails on duplicate normative identity ownership, malformed Markdown tables, missing exact registry targets, stale current revision/lock references, wildcard or approximate stable targets, missing required Canonical Space Type fields, invalid Layer 1/Layer 2 counts, or authorization leakage. (Historical note: Revision 18's own validation pass, which this Contract's validation rule previously described in the present tense, is preserved as historical fact in Annex AF/AE and is no longer the current-tense subject of this rule.)

Historical references are permitted only when explicitly labelled historical.

## 8. Dependency and candidate-lock prerequisites

### 8.1 Dependency role

Contract 1 is a vocabulary root for Contracts 2, 3, 6, 7, 8, 9 and 10.
References to future contracts are ownership boundaries, not reverse
normative dependencies.

### 8.2 Content readiness invariants

```text
non-space Master concepts: 97
Platform Domains: 16
Facility Contexts: 76
Space Families: 75 (71 from Revision 18, plus 4 new residential families)
Layer 1 Canonical Space Types: 206 (195 from Revision 18, plus 11 new residential types, including space.residential.kitchen_living_room)
Layer 2-selected Canonical Space Types: 143 (132 from Revision 18, plus the 11 new residential types)
Space Subtypes: 3 (0 from Revision 18; 3 new residential subtypes added by Revision 19)
Composite Space Profiles (metadata records, Annex AG): 1 (0 from Revision 18; describes the canonical space.residential.kitchen_living_room, does not add a 35th category)
Functional Zones: 43
Space Uses: 62
Components: 4
Layer 2 Activation entries: 304 (285 from Revision 18, plus 19 new entries)
Facility-to-Space applicability rows: 1066 (1013 from Revision 18, plus 53 new residential rows)
Space-to-Zone applicability rows: 57
Space Use applicability rows: 89
duplicate normative Canonical Space Type owners: 0
missing Platform Domain references: 0
missing Facility/Family/Space/Zone/Use targets: 0
```

### 8.3 Candidate-lock prerequisites

```text
candidateLockId:
C1-REV19-CL-001 — reserved, not issued (draft record prepared
alongside this document)
```

Issuance requires:

1. one complete end-to-end consolidated review of the exact Revision 19 artifact;
2. closure of the complete findings set against that exact hash;
3. explicit Project Owner designation of locking authority;
4. exact Revision 19 SHA-256 reconfirmation at issuance time;
5. no content change after the reviewed hash is fixed.

No historical Candidate Lock, including `C1-REV18-CL-001`, may be reused as the Revision 19 lock (consistent with `C1-REV18-CL-001`'s own Binding Note 2).

### 8.4 Frozen scope

Upon issuance, `C1-REV19-CL-001` freezes the entire exact Revision 19 file, including all body sections and every annex from A0 through AI.

The lock is a whole-file hash boundary. No annex, row, historical projection or candidate-status record is excluded.

---

## 10. Resolved architectural boundaries

### 10.1 `toilet_room`

The semantic identity is resolved. Runtime emission remains prohibited until separate runtime alignment and implementation authorization.

### 10.2 Escalation

A contradiction with an accepted baseline is escalated and never silently redefined.

### 10.3 Sanitary-room classification — atomic supersession, now aligned with the in-place corrected Bounded Scope Rev5

```text
toilet_room: toilet present; bathtub and bathing shower absent; hygienic spray allowed.
bathroom: bathtub present; bathing shower optional; toilet absent.
shower_room: bathing shower present; bathtub and toilet absent.
combined_bathroom: toilet present together with bathtub and/or bathing shower.
```

These definitions (unchanged in substance from Revision 18) are now directly consistent with Candidate A Bounded Scope Decision Rev5, Section 7A (in-place corrected, commit 565a3a03294086f319ccec5ff2e77afb5af8a9e1), which independently states the identical four-category model. They become fully effective (`active_locked`) only upon explicit Project Owner acceptance of the complete Atomic Root-Transition Package (Bounded Scope + Contract 1 Revision 19 + Evaluation Threshold Plan successor + Test Data Handling successor + Module Applicability Profile successor; Section 1; Annex J.3) — not with a future Bounded Scope "successor," since Bounded Scope Rev5 itself was corrected in place, not superseded by a new revision.

### 10.4 `toilet_room` history

Its prior accepted scope and evaluation-methodology status remain historical facts. Corpus, provider/model evaluation, implementation and production remain unauthorized.

## 11. Atomic package decision entries

Atomic root-transition acceptance must confirm:

1. Revision 19 as the Contract 1 package candidate.
2. `C1-REV12-CL-001` remains an issued historical lock only; `C1-REV18-CL-001` remains an issued, not repository-persisted lock, superseded as the active candidate by this Revision 19.
3. all 206 Layer 1 Canonical Space Types and all 16 Platform Domains are governed by the global registries;
4. exactly 143 Canonical Space Types and 304 identities remain selected in Layer 2 Candidate, all at `active_candidate` for the 34 residential room categories (`space.residential.kitchen_living_room` included among them, not a 35th) and the separate Composite Space Profile metadata record (Annex AG);
5. `active_locked` remains withheld from every identity until explicit Project Owner acceptance of the complete Atomic Root-Transition Package (Annex J.3);
6. the sanitary-room and activation-ownership supersessions become effective only atomically;
7. the complete Environment/Enclosure and Attachment Mode assignments;
8. the complete runtime-binding gap model, including the Revision 19 resolution of `dining_room` and `home_office`, while `library` and `terrace` remain explicit no-exact-target legacy values;
9. the Composite Space Profile Registry (Annex AG) and Track E Compatibility Identity Hooks (Annex AH), both additive and non-activating;
10. no corpus, provider evaluation, implementation or production authorization.

## 12. Explicit non-authorization boundary

Revision 19 does not authorize repository persistence, candidate-lock issuance, Layer 2 effective activation (`active_locked`), Track E activation, runtime or schema changes beyond those explicitly recorded in Annex AF, corpus/annotation work, provider/model evaluation, implementation, production, Contract 3 drafting, Diagnosability Architecture or Security Architecture.

# Annex A0 — Active Space Registries

## A0.1 Space Master reading projection (non-normative)

This table is a convenience projection of the historical five-category Layer 2 Operational room identities (Revision 18 and earlier). Section 2.3 above is the current, normative statement of the 34-category operational profile as of Revision 19; this table is not updated row-by-row for all 34 because it is explicitly non-normative and does not carry contractual effect.

The sole normative owner of every Canonical Space Type identity, parent family, lifecycle state and discriminative definition is Annex S. The sole normative owner of every Space Subtype is Annex S1. The sole normative owner of the Composite Space Profile is Annex AG. Annex A0.3 owns the current normative Residential-34 active-profile localization projection; as of the Phase 4 final narrow closure pass, Annex A0.3 provides complete Residential-34 RU localization for all 34 active categories (34/34) — the former 15-row gap (Annex AF item 8, historical) is closed and is not a current condition. Addition count for Revision 19: 10 canonical + 3 specialization + 1 composite-described category = 14 total user-facing identity additions; the composite-described category, kitchen_living_room, is one of the 14, not an additional item beyond them. Editing this table alone has no contractual effect.

|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| space.residential.living_room | Annex S | Living Room | Гостиная | Қонақ бөлме | sp.candidate-a.five-room.1.0.0-candidate |
| space.residential.bedroom | Annex S | Bedroom | Спальня | Жатын бөлме | sp.candidate-a.five-room.1.0.0-candidate |
| space.residential.kitchen | Annex S | Kitchen | Кухня | Ас үй | sp.candidate-a.five-room.1.0.0-candidate |
| space.residential.bathroom | Annex S | Bathroom | Ванная комната | Жуынатын бөлме | sp.candidate-a.five-room.1.0.0-candidate |
| space.residential.toilet_room | Annex S | Toilet Room | Туалетная комната | Дәретхана | sp.candidate-a.five-room.1.0.0-candidate |

All 34 rows above (`c1.space.001`–`c1.space.034`) are Revision 19's complete, closed Residential-34 SpaceProfileBinding Registry — exactly one row per active room category, all versioned under `sp.candidate-a.residential-34.1.0.0-candidate`. The historical `sp.candidate-a.five-room.1.0.0-candidate` token no longer appears as a current row version anywhere in this registry; it survives only as explicit historical migration provenance text within the evidence-snapshot column of rows `c1.space.001`–`c1.space.005`. No row collapses a subtype (`children_room`, `guest_bedroom`, `primary_bedroom`) into `bedroom`, or the composite-described canonical (`kitchen_living_room`) into `living_room`/`kitchen` — each of the 34 rows exports its own, distinct downstream identity.

## A0.3 Active Space Localization Registry

**Localization ownership and fallback rule (Revision 19):**

```text
Annex U1 is the sole normative EN-label source for the identity kinds
actually represented by that registry: Facility Context,
Canonical Space Type, Space Subtype and Composite Space Profile.
It does not claim ownership of Platform Domain, Space Family,
Functional Zone, Space Use, Component or every other Layer 1 identity.

Annex A0.3 is the sole normative Residential-34 display-localization
projection. It provides EN 34/34 and RU 34/34 for the active room
categories.

The platform-level fallback rule follows the accepted Vision
Architecture: when a requested non-EN locale unit is unavailable,
canonical EN is the mandatory fallback. Fallback is never silent:
requestedLocale, resolvedLocale, fallbackApplied and localizationStatus
must be preserved in the caller's trace or audit envelope.

KK coverage remains exactly as it existed before this correction:
five localized values are preserved. The other 29 categories have an
empty KK label and localizationStatus = not_yet_localized. No fabricated
or machine-inferred KK translation is introduced.
```

The `Target external SpaceType token` column is a serialization/alignment target and names the bare proposed external literal corresponding to the exact `RoomCategoryIdentityId` in Annex S or Annex S1. For example, `kitchen_living_room` corresponds to `space.residential.kitchen_living_room`; the column never contains a `composite.*` Annex AG metadata identifier. Semantic identity remains governed by the room-category identity and is never inferred from this wire-target column.

This registry contains exactly 34 rows — one per active residential room category. EN 34/34 and RU 34/34 are localized. KK preserves five pre-existing labels unchanged; the remaining 29 rows have an empty KK label (`—`) and the separate governed status `not_yet_localized`. When any requested non-EN locale unit is unavailable, canonical EN is used as the mandatory, explicitly traced fallback required by the accepted Vision Architecture.

Residential-34 room aliases (EN and RU, deterministic text resolution) are governed in Annex U2, not here and not in Annex D — this registry (Annex A0.3) owns only the localized display labels above, not alias/text-resolution rows.

# Annex A4 — ContextSelection Registries

## A4.1 ContextSelectionMember Registry

| Binding ID | Context selection set | Master concept | Context condition | Selected profile concept | Resolution | Required semantic evidence | Registry version |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| c1.context.041 | c1.context_set.sink_space | entity.fixed_element.sanitary.sink | resolvedRoomContext = profile.space.kitchen or profile.space.kitchen_living_room | profile.candidate_a.sink_kitchen | deterministic_when_condition_satisfied | validated resolved-room-context reference | br.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate |
| c1.context.048 | c1.context_set.sink_space | entity.fixed_element.sanitary.sink | resolvedRoomContext = profile.space.bathroom or profile.space.toilet_room or profile.space.shower_room or profile.space.combined_bathroom | profile.candidate_a.sink_bathroom | deterministic_when_condition_satisfied | validated resolved-room-context reference | br.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate |

## A4.2 ContextSelectionSet Registry

| ContextSelectionSet ID | Master concept | Member binding IDs | Resolution cardinality | Unknown-context failure code | Unknown-context outcome | No-match failure code | No-match semantic outcome | Ambiguous failure code | Ambiguous outcome | Invariant | Registry version |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| c1.context_set.sink_space | entity.fixed_element.sanitary.sink | c1.context.041; c1.context.048 | exactly_one_when_supported_room_context_is_known | CTX_CONTEXT_UNKNOWN | mappingStatus: unknown_due_to_evidence — the governed room context cannot be determined | CTX_NO_MATCH | mappingStatus: known_but_unbound — the governed room context is known, but no registered ContextSelectionMember supports it | CTX_AMBIGUOUS | mappingStatus: unresolved — more than one member condition is satisfied | Atypical room placement does not invalidate the Master concept; unknown room context is never conflated with a known unsupported room context | br.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate |

`Unknown-context` applies only when the governed room context cannot be resolved from evidence. `No-match` applies only when
the governed room context is resolved and known, but does not satisfy any
member condition of this set. These are disjoint conditions and never
share a failure code.

# Annex B — Active Evaluation Profile Registry

**Revision 19 note (not remediated by this revision, explicitly classified as KNOWN NON-BLOCKING HISTORICAL/ADVISORY DRIFT, not a Contract-level blocker):** Annex B governs Object/StructuralElement active-profile identities, not room-type identity. Every row in this registry's `Typical spaces (advisory)` column, and every row's `Profile version` cell (`vp.candidate-a.five-room.1.0.0-candidate`), still reflect only the historical five-category residential model (`living_room`, `bedroom`, `kitchen`, `bathroom`, `toilet_room`). The `Typical spaces` column is explicitly advisory, not a normative applicability constraint (its own column header says so); its historical five-room literal/version is an immutable historical object-profile identifier, not the current room-category profile. This drift does not limit Residential-34 room recognition and does not alter Annex A0.2/Annex U2/Annex AA room closure (all of which are complete for all 34 categories, per Annex AF). Updating dozens of advisory rows to reflect all 34 categories is a materially larger task than the governing Phase 4 instruction's explicit scope and remains outside Phase 4. This is the sole known non-blocking drift item disclosed by this Contract; the former Annex A0.3 15-row gap referenced in earlier drafting passes is closed (Annex AF item 8) and must not be read as still current.

The fallback columns use only Revision 12 terminal semantics. Unknown
evidence produces `unknown_due_to_evidence`; a known Master concept with
no applicable current binding produces `known_but_unbound` with
`KNOWN_UNBOUND_EXCLUDED`.

| Profile entry | Active profile concept | Master concept | Profile activation | Node class | Wire literal | Schema representability | Serialization role | Semantic identity exported to downstream evaluation | Semantic parent | Serialization collapse target | Executable fallback | Unknown behavior | Known-unbound behavior | Typical spaces (advisory) | Prohibited spaces | Localization | Implementation readiness | Runtime emission authorization | Profile version |

# Annex F — DisambiguationRule Registry

**Candidate-set invariant.** A rule's registered candidate universe may
serve multiple contextual aliases. For a specific invocation, the rule
may return only a candidate contained in that invoking
`ContextualAliasEntry`. Returning any other candidate is invalid.

**Single-result invariant.** One `NormalizationRecord` represents one
independently observed entity, component or room. When one phrase or
provider response refers to multiple independently visible entities,
normalization produces one record per entity under the caller's batch
envelope. A single record never resolves to multiple `masterConceptId`
values.

| Rule ID | Registered candidate universe | Required semantic evidence | Decision conditions | Priority | Ambiguous outcome | Ambiguous failure code | Unknown outcome | Unknown failure code | Ownership boundary | Registry version |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| c1.disambiguation.installation_state | entity.fixed_element.millwork.built_in_cabinetry; entity.furniture.storage.wardrobe_freestanding | visible attachment to architecture; floor/wall integration; independent mobility evidence | built-in evidence → FixedElement; freestanding/movable evidence → Furniture | installation-state evidence before room context | mappingStatus: unresolved | DISAMBIG_INSTALLATION_AMBIGUOUS | mappingStatus: unknown_due_to_evidence | DISAMBIG_INSTALLATION_INSUFFICIENT | Contract 1 owns semantic branch conditions only; evidence and adjudication schemas remain downstream-owned | dr.candidate-a.1.0.0-candidate |
| c1.disambiguation.assembly_boundary | entity.fixed_element.millwork.bathroom_cabinetry; entity.fixed_element.sanitary.sink; entity.interior_accessory.mirror | independent visible boundaries; plumbing function; storage body; reflective panel identity | normalize each independently observed assembly in its own record; never substitute one component for the whole assembly | entity boundary before lexical preference | mappingStatus: unresolved | DISAMBIG_ASSEMBLY_AMBIGUOUS | mappingStatus: unknown_due_to_evidence | DISAMBIG_ASSEMBLY_INSUFFICIENT | Contract 1 owns semantic branch conditions only; evidence and adjudication schemas remain downstream-owned | dr.candidate-a.1.0.0-candidate |
| c1.disambiguation.appliance_boundary | entity.equipment.appliance.range_combined; entity.equipment.appliance.cooktop; entity.equipment.appliance.oven | visible oven cavity; cooktop surface; unified or separate enclosure | unified cooktop+oven body → range_combined; surface only → cooktop; cavity only → oven | physical assembly boundary before provider wording | mappingStatus: unresolved | DISAMBIG_APPLIANCE_AMBIGUOUS | mappingStatus: unknown_due_to_evidence | DISAMBIG_APPLIANCE_INSUFFICIENT | Contract 1 owns semantic branch conditions only; evidence and adjudication schemas remain downstream-owned | dr.candidate-a.1.0.0-candidate |
| c1.disambiguation.lighting_identity | entity.lighting.generic; entity.lighting.portable.floor_lamp; entity.lighting.portable.table_lamp; entity.lighting.ceiling.pendant; entity.lighting.ceiling.chandelier; entity.lighting.ceiling.flush_mount; entity.lighting.ceiling.recessed; entity.lighting.wall.sconce | mounting position; base/stand; ceiling/wall integration; visible luminaire boundary | exact diagnostic identity when determinable; otherwise governed collapse to light_fixture only after Master identity resolution | exact diagnostic identity before generic lighting | mappingStatus: unresolved | DISAMBIG_LIGHTING_AMBIGUOUS | mappingStatus: unknown_due_to_evidence | DISAMBIG_LIGHTING_INSUFFICIENT | Contract 1 owns semantic branch conditions only; evidence and adjudication schemas remain downstream-owned | dr.candidate-a.1.0.0-candidate |

| projectionId | historicalIdentity | historicalFact | currentNormativeOwner | migrationStatus |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| projection.rev12.living_room | space.residential.living_room | existed in Revision 12 operational vocabulary | Annex S | migrated_preserving_stable_id |
| projection.rev12.bedroom | space.residential.bedroom | existed in Revision 12 operational vocabulary | Annex S | migrated_preserving_stable_id |
| projection.rev12.kitchen | space.residential.kitchen | existed in Revision 12 operational vocabulary | Annex S | migrated_preserving_stable_id |
| projection.rev12.bathroom | space.residential.bathroom | existed in Revision 12; definition deliberately superseded by Owner Decision | Annex S | migrated_preserving_stable_id |
| projection.rev12.toilet_room | space.residential.toilet_room | accepted after the original four-room baseline and preserved with prior scope/evaluation history | Annex S | migrated_preserving_stable_id |

| projection.rev16.combined_bathroom | space.residential.combined_bathroom | introduced after Revision 12 by the sanitary-room successor decision | Annex S | new_identity_not_historical_rev12 |

No nonexistent `profile.space.shower_room` or `profile.space.combined_bathroom` target is implied. Both identities remain Layer 2 Candidate entries only.

# Annex H — Master Boundary and Semantic Rule Registries

## H.1 Generic Semantic Rule Registry

| Rule ID | Rule kind | Normative rule | Applies to |
|---|---|---|---|
| rule.aggregate.descendant_membership | inclusion | aggregation/root concept is valid only as the semantic parent of registered descendants | aggregation/root |
| rule.aggregate.not_directly_emittable | exclusion | aggregation/root concept is not directly emitted without an explicit Active Profile or diagnostic-collapse binding | aggregation/root |
| rule.space.authoritative_type | inclusion | space identity is supplied by a governed SpaceType reference, a deterministic registered space alias, or an explicitly accepted annotation | space |
| rule.space.no_silent_substitution | exclusion | one room type must not be silently substituted for another | space |
| rule.diagnostic.visible_identity | inclusion | diagnostic concept requires direct visible semantic evidence | diagnostic |
| rule.diagnostic.no_guess | exclusion | diagnostic identity is not inferred when evidence is insufficient | diagnostic |
| rule.sanitary.basin_plumbing_evidence | inclusion | one installed sanitary basin/plumbing fixture is identified independently of room context | sanitary |
| rule.sanitary.room_context_not_master_identity | exclusion | room-specific selection is never part of Master sink identity; it is resolved only by the ContextSelectionSet | sanitary |

## H.2 MasterConceptBoundary Registry

This registry is profile-independent and keyed exclusively by
`MasterConceptId`. Active Profile entries inherit these boundaries and
may add serialization/context constraints, but cannot redefine Master
identity.

| entity.opening.door | boundary.identity.door | one operable door leaf or door assembly | boundary.include.door | visible door leaf/frame or unmistakable closure function | boundary.exclude.door | exclude doorless openings and decorative wall panels | bd.candidate-a.1.0.0-candidate |

| entity.opening.generic | boundary.identity.opening | one structural passage without a separately classified door/window | boundary.include.opening | visible pass-through boundary or archway | boundary.exclude.opening | exclude shadows, wall art and occlusion gaps | bd.candidate-a.1.0.0-candidate |

# Annex I — Contract-1 Code Registries

## I.1 FailureCode Registry

Registry version:

```text
fc.candidate-a.1.0.0-candidate
```

A `NormalizationRecord` carries at most one primary `failureCode`. The
primary code is selected by the stage that produced the terminal
`mappingStatus`.

- `INPUT_WIRE_UNKNOWN` applies when a trusted literal is not a member of
  the declared profile version; `W2P_UNKNOWN_LITERAL` applies only after
  membership succeeds but node-class-scoped reverse resolution is absent.
- `INPUT_LEGACY_UNSUPPORTED` applies when the legacy artifact's own
  recorded version has no supported read path; `UNSUPPORTED_VOCABULARY_VERSION`
  applies to another consulted registry-version mismatch.
- Ambiguous disambiguation and insufficient-evidence disambiguation use
  different codes and different terminal outcomes.

| Code | Severity | Retryable | Blocking scope | Meaning | Required terminal outcome |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| INPUT_WIRE_UNKNOWN | blocking | no | normalization | Trusted wire literal is absent from the declared profile version | invalid |
| INPUT_PROVIDER_UNMAPPED | diagnostic | yes | normalization | Provider label has no deterministic/contextual Master mapping | unknown_due_to_evidence |
| INPUT_ANNOTATION_UNMAPPED | diagnostic | yes | normalization | Annotation text has no Master mapping | unknown_due_to_evidence |
| INPUT_USER_UNMAPPED | diagnostic | yes | normalization | User text has no Master mapping | unknown_due_to_evidence |
| INPUT_LEGACY_UNSUPPORTED | blocking | no | normalization | Legacy artifact's own recorded registry version has no supported direct read or authorized adapter | unsupported_version |
| DISAMBIG_INSTALLATION_AMBIGUOUS | diagnostic | yes | normalization | Multiple installation-state candidates remain viable | unresolved |
| DISAMBIG_INSTALLATION_INSUFFICIENT | diagnostic | yes | normalization | Installation-state evidence is insufficient | unknown_due_to_evidence |
| DISAMBIG_ASSEMBLY_AMBIGUOUS | diagnostic | yes | normalization | Multiple assembly-boundary candidates remain viable for one independently observed entity | unresolved |
| DISAMBIG_ASSEMBLY_INSUFFICIENT | diagnostic | yes | normalization | Assembly-boundary evidence is insufficient | unknown_due_to_evidence |
| DISAMBIG_APPLIANCE_AMBIGUOUS | diagnostic | yes | normalization | Multiple appliance-boundary candidates remain viable | unresolved |
| DISAMBIG_APPLIANCE_INSUFFICIENT | diagnostic | yes | normalization | Appliance-boundary evidence is insufficient | unknown_due_to_evidence |
| DISAMBIG_LIGHTING_AMBIGUOUS | diagnostic | yes | normalization | Multiple lighting identities remain viable | unresolved |
| DISAMBIG_LIGHTING_INSUFFICIENT | diagnostic | yes | normalization | Lighting evidence is insufficient | unknown_due_to_evidence |
| M2P_NO_MATCH | blocking | no | candidate_lock/normalization | A Master concept referenced by an ActiveProfileEntry and requiring a direct MasterToProfileBinding lacks that binding | candidate-lock failure or known_but_unbound during normalization |
| P2W_INVALID | blocking | no | candidate_lock/normalization | ProfileToWire binding violates node/literal/cardinality invariants | candidate-lock failure; emission prohibited |
| W2P_UNKNOWN_LITERAL | blocking | no | normalization | Literal is present in the declared profile version but lacks node-class-scoped WireToProfileResolution | invalid |
| CTX_CONTEXT_UNKNOWN | diagnostic | yes | normalization | Governed room context cannot be resolved | unknown_due_to_evidence |
| CTX_NO_MATCH | diagnostic | yes | normalization | Governed room context is known but satisfies no member of the invoked context set | known_but_unbound |
| CTX_AMBIGUOUS | diagnostic | yes | normalization | Multiple context members remain viable | unresolved |
| COLLAPSE_TARGET_MISSING | blocking | no | candidate_lock/normalization | Diagnostic collapse target is missing or not active in the declared profile | candidate-lock failure; normalization prohibited |
| MASTER_ID_DUP | blocking | no | candidate_lock | Stable Master ID is duplicated or reused | candidate-lock failure |
| MASTER_PARENT_INVALID | blocking | no | candidate_lock | Master parent is missing or hierarchy contains a cycle | candidate-lock failure |
| MASTER_TYPE_INVALID | blocking | no | candidate_lock | Master concept type is absent or invalid | candidate-lock failure |
| MASTER_FAMILY_INVALID | blocking | no | candidate_lock | Semantic family is absent or invalid | candidate-lock failure |
| MASTER_LABEL_MISSING | blocking | no | candidate_lock | Required canonical label is absent | candidate-lock failure |
| MASTER_BOUNDARY_MISSING | blocking | no | candidate_lock | Required Master boundary summary/rule is absent | candidate-lock failure |
| MASTER_INCLUSION_INVALID | blocking | no | candidate_lock | Inclusion-rule reference is absent or invalid | candidate-lock failure |
| MASTER_EXCLUSION_INVALID | blocking | no | candidate_lock | Exclusion-rule reference is absent or invalid | candidate-lock failure |
| MASTER_INSTALLATION_INVALID | blocking | no | candidate_lock | Installation applicability contradicts semantic identity | candidate-lock failure |
| MASTER_ASSEMBLY_INVALID | blocking | no | candidate_lock | Assembly policy contradicts concept/component identity | candidate-lock failure |
| MASTER_LIFECYCLE_INVALID | blocking | no | candidate_lock/versioning | Lifecycle state or transition is invalid | candidate-lock failure or successor required |
| MASTER_VERSION_MISSING | blocking | no | candidate_lock/versioning | Required registry version is absent | candidate-lock failure |
| MASTER_DEPRECATION_INVALID | blocking | no | candidate_lock/versioning | Deprecation metadata is invalid | candidate-lock failure |
| MASTER_REPLACEMENT_INVALID | blocking | no | candidate_lock/versioning | Replacement ID is invalid | candidate-lock failure |
| P2W_LITERAL_DUP | blocking | no | candidate_lock | Node-class/wire pair has multiple Active Profile owners | candidate-lock failure |
| KNOWN_UNBOUND_EXCLUDED | diagnostic | no | normalization | Known Master concept has no applicable authorized Active Profile binding in the current profile/context | known_but_unbound |
| UNSUPPORTED_VOCABULARY_VERSION | blocking | no | normalization/versioning | A consulted RegistryVersionSet entry is unavailable or mismatched | unsupported_version |
| ALIAS_COLLISION | blocking | no | candidate_lock/normalization | Scoped deterministic alias has competing Master targets | candidate-lock failure |

## I.2 ExternalAlignmentRequirementCode Registry

Registry version:

```text
arc.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate
```

This is the first explicitly governed version of this code registry and
supersedes the unversioned historical single-row projection retained in
Revision 18.

These codes never populate `NormalizationRecord.failureCode` and never
change semantic `mappingStatus`. They record external runtime work. The
`Applies to` expression is evaluated against Annex A0.2 and is not a
single-row example.

| Code | Applies to | Meaning | Semantic status | Blocking scope |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| SPACE_TYPE_EXTERNAL_MISSING | every Annex A0.2 row where `alignmentStatus = registry_extension_required` | the semantic `RoomCategoryIdentityId` and proposed `TargetExternalSpaceTypeId` are resolved, but that target token is absent from the imported runtime SpaceType registry | resolved | runtime_alignment only |

---

# Annex J — Lifecycle, Profile Activation, Deprecation, Migration and Reserved Foundations

## J.1 MasterLifecycleStatus Registry

The authoritative Master lifecycle enum is:

```text
reserved | experimental | active | deprecated | retired
```

| Status | Meaning | New Active Profile binding allowed |
|---|---|---|
| reserved | stable ID allocated; concrete identity not yet approved for use | no |
| experimental | concrete Master identity under governed architecture development | no |
| active | valid Master identity eligible for governed profile binding | yes, by separate profile decision |
| deprecated | historical identity remains readable; no new binding or new production use | no |
| retired | terminal historical identity retained only for backward reading/audit | no |

## J.2 MasterLifecycleTransition Registry

| From | To | Allowed | Authority | Required record |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| reserved | experimental | allowed | Project Owner-authorized Contract 1 successor | VocabularyChangeRecord |
| experimental | active | allowed | Project Owner architecture/package decision | VocabularyChangeRecord |
| experimental | reserved | allowed only before any external use | Project Owner-authorized Contract 1 successor | VocabularyChangeRecord |
| active | deprecated | allowed | Project Owner accepted successor version | DeprecationRecord + VocabularyChangeRecord |
| deprecated | retired | allowed | Project Owner accepted successor version | DeprecationRecord update + VocabularyChangeRecord |
| deprecated | active | prohibited | none | new stable ID required |
| retired | any | prohibited | none | new stable ID required |
| any | reserved | prohibited except experimental → reserved above | none | ID reuse prohibited |

All transitions not listed as allowed are prohibited.

## J.3 ProfileActivationStatus and Transition Registry

The authoritative profile activation enum is:

```text
inactive | active_candidate | active_locked | profile_deprecated
```

This axis is independent from Master lifecycle. A Master concept must be
`active` before it can become `active_candidate`, but profile activation
never changes Master lifecycle.

| From | To | Allowed | Authority | Required record |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| inactive | active_candidate | allowed | authorized Supporting Contract drafting decision | ProfileActivationRecord |
| active_candidate | active_locked | allowed | explicit Project Owner acceptance of the complete Atomic Root-Transition Package (Bounded Scope + Contract 1 Revision 19 + Evaluation Threshold Plan successor + Test Data Handling successor + Module Applicability Profile successor) | ProfileActivationRecord |
| active_candidate | inactive | allowed | authorized successor before package acceptance | ProfileActivationRecord |
| active_locked | profile_deprecated | allowed | accepted successor profile/package | ProfileActivationRecord + downstream impact record |
| profile_deprecated | active_locked | prohibited | none | new profile version required |

### J.3.1 ProfileActivationRecord realization

`ProfileActivationRecord` is a logical transition record, not a second
duplicate activation registry. For a registry-version transition, the
authoritative record is the tuple:

```text
prior Layer2ActivationEntry in the immediately preceding profile version
+ current Annex AA Layer2ActivationEntry
+ ownerDecisionReference
+ prior/current profile version tokens
```

For the Revision 19 `inactive → active_candidate` transitions, the
current Annex AA rows under `l2.candidate-a.rev2-candidate`, together
with their predecessor rows under `l2.candidate-a.rev1-candidate` and
the recorded Owner authorization, constitute the required
`ProfileActivationRecord`. The current row's
`effectiveActivationStatus` is the `toStatus`; the predecessor row's
status is the `fromStatus`. No separate duplicated row is permitted.
`active_locked` still requires a later explicit Owner acceptance of the
complete Atomic Root-Transition Package and a new profile-version
transition.

## J.4 VocabularyChangeClass Registry

| Change class | Examples | Version effect | Required review/decision |
|---|---|---|---|
| patch | prose/label correction with no identity, boundary or mapping effect | patch | administrative verification |
| minor | additive active Master concept; additive alias; additive reserved namespace; non-breaking metadata | minor | architecture impact decision |
| major | split/merge; parent change; identity boundary change; profile identity/wire change; semantic evaluation identity change | major | Project Owner architecture acceptance and downstream revalidation |

## J.5 DeprecationRecord schema and backward-read rule

| Field | Type | Required | Invariant |
|---|---|---|---|
| deprecatedId | StableId | yes | existing Master concept |
| deprecatedInVersion | VersionToken | yes | not earlier than introducedVersion |
| replacementId | StableId/null | conditional | required when a canonical successor exists |
| migrationType | enum | yes | rename, split, merge, terminal |
| migrationRuleId | RuleId/null | conditional | required for rename/split/merge |
| contractualReadPath | enum | yes | read_directly, read_via_versioned_adapter |
| externalResourceAvailability | enum | yes | available, unavailable |

Contract 1 guarantees that sufficient identity/version/migration metadata
is preserved to select `read_directly` or `read_via_versioned_adapter`.
An actual runtime read may end as `unsupported_version` only when a
required historical resource outside Contract 1 ownership is unavailable.
This is recorded separately as `externalResourceAvailability:
unavailable`; it does not erase the contractual read-path metadata and
never permits silent reinterpretation.

## J.6 Namespace Status Registry

Namespace status is a governance state, not a Master lifecycle state.

```text
reserved → populated: allowed when a concrete normative registry is created
reserved → deprecated_alias: allowed when an unused namespace is superseded
populated → reserved: prohibited
deprecated_alias → populated: prohibited; use the canonical replacement namespace
```

| namespacePattern | domain | status | normativeOwnerOrPurpose | Registry version |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| space.* | canonical space | populated | Annex S | ns.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate |
| facility.* | facility context | populated | Annex Q | ns.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate |
| family.* | space family | populated | Annex R | ns.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate |
| zone.* | functional zone | populated | Annex T | ns.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate |
| use.* | space use | populated | Annex U | ns.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate |
| shared.* | shared concept | populated | Annex Y | ns.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate |
| component.* | component | populated | Annex S0 | ns.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate |
| composition.* | property composition | populated | Annex O | ns.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate |
| environment.* | environment/enclosure | populated | Annex P.1 | ns.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate |
| attachment.* | attachment mode | populated | Annex P.2 | ns.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate |
| binding.* | runtime binding | populated | Annex AB | ns.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate |
| space.wellness.* | legacy namespace | deprecated_alias | replacement: space.wellness_fitness.* | ns.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate |
| material.* | material intelligence | reserved | future separately authorized Color & Material foundation | ns.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate |
| finish.* | finish intelligence | reserved | future separately authorized Color & Material foundation | ns.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate |
| color.* | color identity/reference | reserved | future separately authorized Color & Material foundation | ns.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate |
| color_space.* | color-space/profile identity/reference | reserved | future separately authorized Color & Material foundation | ns.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate |
