# Annex L — Record and Value Schema Registry

Registry version: `rs.candidate-a.4.0.0-candidate` (3.0.0 from Revision 18; bumped by Revision 19 for the new record types and closed-status corrections below)

| Type | Kind | Normative source | Required core fields | Primary invariants |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| MasterVocabularyEntry | record | Annex G | masterConceptId; semanticParentId; conceptType; semanticFamily; identityKind; canonicalLabelEN; boundary/rule/lifecycle fields | non-space identities only; unique stable ID |
| CanonicalSpaceTypeEntry | record | Annex S | all fields listed in §2a.5 | 206 unique IDs (195 from Revision 18, plus 11 new residential types, including space.residential.kitchen_living_room); one normative owner; valid family/domain refs |
| SpaceSubtypeEntry | record | Annex S1 | spaceSubtypeId; parentCanonicalSpaceTypeId; normativeDefinition; lifecycleStatus | empty registry was permitted through Revision 18; as of Revision 19, 3 residential entries populated (children_room, guest_bedroom, primary_bedroom, all parented to space.residential.bedroom); an empty registry remains structurally valid for any future domain that has none |
| RoomCategoryIdentityId | closed union value type | §4.1; Annexes S, S1 and A0.2 | CanonicalSpaceTypeId; SpaceSubtypeId | exact semantic room identity; never a wire literal; specialization identities must not collapse to their canonical parent |
| TargetExternalSpaceTypeId | governed external-alignment token type | §4.1; §5.4; Annex A0.2 | normalized bare token proposed for current or future external SpaceType registry alignment | may equal an existing imported `SpaceTypeId` or name a required future registry-extension token; absence from the current runtime enum is valid only with `registry_extension_required`; never substitutes for `RoomCategoryIdentityId` and is never emitted without alignment plus authorization |
| RegistryVersionSet | version map | §3.3; §5.4 | RegistryId → exact VersionToken/commit for every registry consulted by one operation | operation-specific and immutable once recorded; must include every actually consulted current, auxiliary or historical registry; absence/mismatch yields `unsupported_version` |
| InputLabelSource | closed value type | §4.5 | trusted_internal_wire; external_provider_text; annotation_text; user_text; legacy_artifact | determines preprocessing, resolution path and allowed terminal outcomes |
| MappingStatus | closed value type | §5.4 | resolved; unresolved; unknown_due_to_evidence; known_but_unbound; invalid; unsupported_version | exactly one terminal status; non-resolved statuses require one primary FailureCode; external alignment does not alter semantic status |
| ExternalSpaceTypeAlignmentStatus | closed value type | §4.2; Annex A0.2 | existing_verified; registry_extension_required | `existing_verified` requires a verified current runtime token or governed legacy alias; `registry_extension_required` requires current token = none and an ExternalAlignmentRequirementCode |
| RuntimeEmissionAuthorization | closed value type | §4.2; Annexes A0.2, A2 and B | not_authorized; authorized | semantic resolution never implies emission authorization |
| ProfileSelectionStatus | closed value type | §4.2; Annex AA | owner_selected | selection is independent from effective activation |
| SchemaRepresentability | closed value type | §4.2; Annexes A2 and B | schema_compatible_open_string; governed_space_type_reference | representability does not imply implementation or emission |
| ImplementationReadiness | closed value type | §4.2; Annexes A2 and B | not_implemented; implemented | independent from semantic activation and production authorization |
| MasterLifecycleStatus | closed value type | Annex J.1 | reserved; experimental; active; deprecated; retired | transitions only through Annex J.2 |
| FailureCode | governed code value | Annex I.1 | one exact code from `fc.candidate-a.1.0.0-candidate` | at most one primary code per NormalizationRecord; required for non-resolved terminal outcomes |
| ExternalAlignmentRequirementCode | governed code value | Annex I.2 | SPACE_TYPE_EXTERNAL_MISSING | alignment-only; never populates failureCode or changes mappingStatus |
| CompositeSpaceProfileEntry | record | Annex AG (new, Revision 19) | compositeProfileId; describesCanonicalSpaceTypeId; componentCanonicalSpaceTypeIds; compositionSemantics; evidenceMethodology; dominanceRule; precedenceRule; inheritanceRule; structuredSceneCompatibility; layer2SelectionStatus; lifecycleStatus | auxiliary metadata only; `describesCanonicalSpaceTypeId` must resolve to exactly one Annex S canonical row; `compositeProfileId` is never a Canonical Space Type, `Room.spaceType`, or `CanonicalSpaceTypeId`; exactly one row as of Revision 19 |
| SpaceProfileBinding | record | Annex A0.2 | bindingId; spaceProfileConceptId; roomCategoryIdentityId; profileActivation; spaceProfileIdentityExportedDownstream; currentExternalSpaceTypeId; targetExternalSpaceTypeId; alignmentStatus; externalRequirementCode; runtimeEmission; immutableEvidenceSnapshot; spaceProfileVersion | exactly 34 current Residential-34 rows; `roomCategoryIdentityId` resolves to an Annex S CanonicalSpaceTypeId or Annex S1 SpaceSubtypeId; every row exports its own unique room identity; subtype identities never collapse to `bedroom`; kitchen_living_room never collapses to either component; targetExternalSpaceTypeId is serialization/alignment-only; runtime emission requires separate alignment and authorization |
| ComponentIdentityEntry | record | Annex S0 | componentId; platformDomainId; parentContext; normativeDefinition; layer2SelectionStatus; lifecycleStatus | never a Canonical Space Type |
| PlatformDomainEntry | record | Annex N | platformDomainId; displayName; layer2Status; layer1FoundationRef; lifecycleStatus | 16 unique domains |
| PropertyCompositionEntry | record | Annex O | compositionId; memberPlatformDomainIds; lifecycleStatus | not a Platform Domain |
| SpaceEnvironmentEntry | record | Annex P.1 | spaceEnvironmentType; description | orthogonal axis token |
| AttachmentModeEntry | record | Annex P.2 | attachmentMode; description | orthogonal axis token |
| Layer2SelectionStatus | closed value type | §4.2 | owner_selected_not_yet_effective; owner_selected_effective; layer1_defined_not_selected | convenience status never replaces Annex AA selection/effectiveness fields; `owner_selected_effective` added by Revision 19 for the 34 active_candidate residential room categories (`kitchen_living_room` included, not a 35th) and the separate Composite Space Profile metadata record |
| ProfileActivationStatus / effectiveActivationStatus | closed value type | §4.2; Annex J.3 | inactive; active_candidate; active_locked; profile_deprecated | one shared closed enum for both the general axis (Annex J.3) and its per-row Annex AA instantiation (effectiveActivationStatus); no duplicate axis retained as of Revision 19; active_locked withheld from every identity pending explicit Project Owner acceptance of the complete Atomic Root-Transition Package |
| FacilityContextEntry | record | Annex Q | facilityContextId; platformDomainId; layer2SelectionStatus; lifecycleStatus | valid domain ref |
| SpaceFamilyEntry | record | Annex R | spaceFamilyId; platformDomainId; layer2SelectionStatus; lifecycleStatus | valid domain ref; 75 unique IDs (71 from Revision 18, plus 4 new residential supporting families — Section 10 of the Revision 19 correction pass confirms these 4 families are not additional user-facing room categories and do not themselves transition beyond the same owner_selected_not_yet_effective status already used by every other existing residential supporting family) |
| CanonicalSpaceOwnershipMigrationEntry | record | Annex S2 | historicalSource; historicalIdentity; currentNormativeOwner; migrationStatus | no dual normative ownership |
| FunctionalZoneEntry | record | Annex T | functionalZoneId; platformDomainId; applicableSpaceFamilyIds; layer2SelectionStatus; lifecycleStatus | each family ID resolves; many-to-many permitted |
| SpaceUseEntry | record | Annex U | spaceUseId; platformDomainId; layer2SelectionStatus; lifecycleStatus | valid domain ref |
| LocalizationEntry | record | Annex U1 | localizationId; identityId; identityKind; locale; label; status | exact target identity; `status` uses LocalizationEntryLifecycleStatus (`active` or `deprecated`) and is distinct from LocalizationStatus, which describes whether a requested locale value exists |
| ActiveSpaceLocalizationEntry | record | Annex A0.3 | spaceProfileConcept; targetExternalSpaceTypeId; labelEN; labelRU; labelKK; kkLocalizationStatus; spaceProfileVersion | exactly 34 Residential-34 rows; EN/RU localized; KK label is separate from status; governed EN fallback is explicit and traceable |
| LocalizationStatus | closed value type | §4.2; Annex A0.3 | localized; not_yet_localized; not_applicable | describes locale-value availability; a status token is never stored or rendered as a localized label |
| LocalizationEntryLifecycleStatus | closed value type | §4.2; Annex U1 | active; deprecated | lifecycle of a localization row; independent from locale-value availability |
| AliasEntryLifecycleStatus | closed value type | §4.2; Annexes U2, D and E | active; deprecated | lifecycle of an alias row; independent from alias determinism/ambiguity |
| AliasEntryFullPlatform | record | Annex U2 | aliasId; normalizedText; locale; identityKind; platformDomainScope; targetIdentityId; aliasType; status | `status` uses AliasEntryLifecycleStatus; deterministic aliases are unique within `locale + normalizedText + platformDomainScope + identityKind`; identical text across different domains or identity kinds is permitted; ambiguous aliases form governed candidate sets |
| FacilityToSpaceApplicabilityEntry | record | Annex V | facilityContextId; canonicalSpaceTypeId; applicability; conditionExpression; activationRole; evidenceMode | exact IDs; structural applicability separated from activation; 1066 total rows (1013 from Revision 18, plus 53 new residential rows) |
| SpaceToZoneApplicabilityEntry | record | Annex W | functionalZoneId; canonicalSpaceTypeId; facilityContextApplicability; applicability; conditionExpression | exact IDs |
| SpaceUseApplicabilityEntry | record | Annex X | spaceUseId; canonicalSpaceTypeId; facilityContextApplicability; applicability; conditionExpression | exact IDs and no contradiction with Annex V |
| SharedConceptGroupEntry | record | Annex Y | sharedConceptGroupId; resolutionPolicy; memberCanonicalSpaceTypeIds; decisionRationale | members exist in Annex S |
| Layer2ActivationEntry | record | Annex AA | all Annex AA fields including status, version and Owner-decision references | 304 unique entries (285 from Revision 18, plus 19 new); valid qualified domain IDs; exactly one wave each |
| RuntimeSpaceTypeBindingEntry | record | Annex AB.1 | runtimeSpaceTypeId; runtimeValueSemanticKind; targetIdentityId; targetIdentityKind; bindingStatus; reason; replacementRuntimeId; migrationStatus | runtime-token role and target-registry kind are separate; exact target or null; no wildcard/approximate ID; `dining_room` and `home_office` resolved to `canonical_binding_ready`; generic `library` and `terrace` remain `no_exact_target` pending exact Residential-34 runtime alignment |
| PlatformDomainRuntimeBindingEntry | record | Annex AB.2 | runtimeDesignDomainId; targetPlatformDomainIds; mappingCardinality; bindingStatus; reason | contains actual runtime values only; legacy umbrella mapping is explicit |
| RuntimeDomainExtensionRequirementEntry | record | Annex AB.3 | requirementId; targetPlatformDomainId; currentReachability; dedicatedRuntimeValueStatus; reason; implementationAuthorization | gap record is not a runtime binding; no implementation authorization |
| NamespaceStatusEntry | record | Annex J.6 | namespacePattern; domain; status; normativeOwnerOrPurpose; registryVersion | status in reserved/populated/deprecated_alias; governed transitions; every row uses ns.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate |
| TrackECompatibilityHookEntry | record | Annex AH (new, Revision 19) | hookIdentityId; describedFutureRole; currentActivationStatus; boundToCurrentIdentity; activatesOnAuthority | reserved, additive, non-activating; currentActivationStatus in {reserved_not_activated, active_now}; no Track E capability (persistent RoomView, cross-session memory, multi-room execution, Project Memory, whole-home topology, 2.5D/3D/full 3D) is activated by any row |
| CrossCuttingCompatibilityHookEntry | record | Annex AI (new, Phase 4 final correction pass; completed with concrete per-foundation hooks by the Phase 4 final consolidated closure correction pass) | compatibilityHookId; foundationId; ownedByThisContract; describedHook; specificHooksReferenced; provenanceReference; activationStatus; introducedVersion | compatibility_only for every row; no cross-cutting foundation architecture is opened, activated, or implemented by any row; does not contradict or reassign Annex I FailureCode Registry ownership; registry version cc.candidate-a.1.0.0-candidate |
| PreprocessingProfile | record/value profile | §4.6 | preprocessingProfileId; successorOf; orderedOperations; excludedOperations | pp.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate performs normalization only; registry lookup is separate and node-class-specific |
| BindingRegistrySet | versioned registry set | Annexes A1–A5 | MasterToProfileBinding; ProfileToWireBinding; WireToProfileResolution; ContextSelectionMember; ContextSelectionSet; DiagnosticCollapseBinding | all current rows use br.candidate-a.2.0.0-candidate; Residential-34 sink contexts are complete |
| NormalizationRecord | record | §5.4 | all fields listed in §5.4 | one record per entity; terminal outcome consistency |
| VocabularyChangeRecord | record | Annex J.2/J.4; Annex AF for Revision 19 additive changes | changeRecordId; affectedStableIds; changeClass; fromVersion; toVersion; authorityReference; rationale; downstreamImpactReferences | required for governed Master lifecycle or vocabulary changes; stable IDs are never reused; major changes require downstream revalidation |
| DeprecationRecord | record | Annex J.5 | deprecatedId; deprecatedInVersion; replacementId; migrationType; migrationRuleId; contractualReadPath; externalResourceAvailability | required for deprecation/retirement; preserves backward-read path and never permits silent reinterpretation |
| ProfileActivationRecord | logical version-transition record | Annex J.3/J.3.1; Annex AA | activationEntryId; identityId; identityKind; fromStatus; toStatus; priorProfileVersion; currentProfileVersion; authorityReference | realized by predecessor/current Layer2ActivationEntry pair plus Owner reference; no duplicate activation registry; active_locked requires later Atomic Root-Transition Package acceptance |
| CandidateLockReadinessRecord | record | Annex K | candidateLockId; issuanceStatus; reviewStatus; frozenScopeUponIssuance; knownDraftingMarkers; candidateLockPreconditions | non-authoritative snapshot; Revision 19 snapshot is current, Revision 18 snapshot retained as historical (Annex K.1) |

# Annex M — Revision 12 Consolidation Record — historical

Revision 12 is the Project Owner-directed final content successor and
supersedes Revisions 1–11 in full. Its consolidated architecture changes
are preserved here unmodified, as Revision 13's immediate predecessor
record:

1. replaces review-driven lineage with a self-contained final baseline;
2. closes the further-review loop by explicit Owner Decision;
3. defines RegistryVersionSet as a consulted-registry immutable map;
4. defines a complete InputLabelSource × node-class execution matrix;
5. defines a fixed NFC/whitespace/default-case-fold preprocessing profile;
6. separates Room wire and Room text normalization paths;
7. makes `toilet_room` semantically `resolved` while runtime alignment remains pending;
8. separates semantic failure codes from external-alignment requirement codes;
9. separates ambiguous and insufficient-evidence disambiguation outcomes/codes;
10. enforces one NormalizationRecord per independently observed entity;
11. makes Master boundaries profile-independent through Annex H.2;
12. establishes disjoint Master lifecycle and Profile activation enums/transitions;
13. qualifies backward-read metadata versus external historical-resource availability;
14. preserves all eight full-platform namespace reservations with status `reserved`;
15. completes schema coverage for every normative record and inline value type;
16. retains all bounded registries and full Master hierarchy without reducing platform scope;
17. retains all non-authorization boundaries for persistence, implementation, corpus and provider work.

# Annex P — Space Environment/Enclosure and Attachment Mode Registries

## P.1 Space Environment / Enclosure Registry

| spaceEnvironmentType | description |
|---|---|
| indoor | fully enclosed interior space |
| enclosed | enclosed but potentially unfinished/service space |
| semi_enclosed | partially open to exterior |
| attached_outdoor | outdoor space physically attached to a property |
| outdoor | open exterior space |

## P.2 Attachment Mode Registry

| attachmentMode | description |
|---|---|
| integrated | fully part of the main structure |
| attached | physically connected but structurally distinguishable |
| detached | separate structure on the same property |
| shared | shared among multiple units/properties |
| not_applicable | attachment relation is not applicable |

# Annex R — Global Space Family Registry

Global Layer 1 registry. 75 Space Families (71 from Revision 18, plus 4 new residential families added by Revision 19: family.residential.below_grade, family.residential.dining, family.residential.utility, family.residential.work_study).

**Explicit confirmation (Phase 4 final correction pass, item 10):** these 4 new families follow exactly the same `layer2SelectionStatus` status model already used by every other existing residential supporting family (`owner_selected_not_yet_effective`, unchanged by Revision 19 for any family row, new or pre-existing). They are Contract-1-owned grouping/supporting identities, not additional user-facing room categories, and are not counted among, and do not expand, the 34 active_candidate residential room categories (Section 2.3). No identity kind other than the 34 room categories and the Composite Space Profile metadata record transitions to `active_candidate` by this revision; Space Families, Facility Contexts, and every other supporting identity kind remain at their pre-existing status, exactly as before Revision 19.

# Annex S1 — Space Subtype Registry

Three Space Subtype identities added by Revision 19, all specializations of `space.residential.bedroom` (Annex S). This registry does not carry its own `layer2SelectionStatus`/`ProfileActivationStatus` column; Layer 2 Candidate membership and Profile Activation status for these three subtypes are recorded in Annex AA (Layer 2 Activation Registry), consistent with how every other Layer 2 Candidate identity kind is activated in this Contract.

| spaceSubtypeId | parentCanonicalSpaceTypeId | normativeDefinition | lifecycleStatus |
|---|---|---|---|
| subtype.residential.children_room | space.residential.bedroom | A bedroom specialized for use by a child, distinguished by child-oriented furniture, décor or fixtures, without changing the parent bedroom's core positive/negative evidence definition. | active |
| subtype.residential.guest_bedroom | space.residential.bedroom | A bedroom specialized for occasional or temporary occupancy by a visiting guest rather than a permanent resident, without changing the parent bedroom's core positive/negative evidence definition. | active |
| subtype.residential.primary_bedroom | space.residential.bedroom | The principal bedroom of a residential unit, typically associated with the primary occupant(s) and, where present, an ensuite or adjoining sanitary/dressing space, without changing the parent bedroom's core positive/negative evidence definition. | active |

Determining which of these three specializations (if any) applies to a given classified `bedroom` instance is a semantic-case/annotation question, not a scope-definition rule, and is not decided by this Contract — consistent with the treatment of the sanitary classification precedence rule (Annex Z §Z.2) as a scope-definition boundary distinct from annotation-contract content.

### S1.1 Applicability closure (Phase 4 final correction pass)

None of the three subtypes above has, or requires, its own row in Annex T (Functional Zone), Annex U (Space Use), Annex V (Facility-to-Space Applicability), Annex W (Space-to-Zone Applicability), or Annex X (Space Use Applicability). This is explicit inherited applicability, not a silent omission:

```text
Inherited applicability rule:
Each of the three subtypes inherits, in full and without exception,
every Facility Context (Annex V), Functional Zone (Annex T/W) and
Space Use (Annex U/X) applicability already recorded for its parent
canonical identity, space.residential.bedroom.

Precedence:
The parent's applicability rows (Annex V, five residential facility
contexts) govern; no subtype-specific override, narrowing, or
exception exists.

Exceptions:
None. No subtype currently narrows or excludes any facility context,
zone, or use available to space.residential.bedroom.

Not-applicable ownership:
Applicability-registry ownership (Annex V/T/U/W/X) itself is not
affected — Annex S remains the sole normative owner of
space.residential.bedroom's own applicability rows; Annex S1 owns
only the subtype identity and its normative definition, never a
duplicate or competing applicability row.

Evidence inheritance:
Positive/negative evidence for same-room-type determination is the
parent's (space.residential.bedroom, Annex S); distinguishing which
subtype (if any) applies is additional, subtype-specific evidence
layered on top of, not replacing, the parent's evidence, and is a
semantic-case/annotation question (paragraph above), not decided
here.

Exact authoritative source:
space.residential.bedroom's own Annex S row and its five Annex V
rows are the sole authoritative source for all facility/zone/use
applicability inherited by all three subtypes.
```

This closure applies identically to all three subtypes; no subtype-by-subtype variation exists as of Revision 19.

The Russian token `салон` is intentionally non-deterministic. All rows with `aliasType = ambiguous_facility_alias` form one contextual candidate set per locale. Resolution requires supplied domain/facility context and must never default to `beauty_salon`.

Rows `alias.full.0282`–`alias.full.0333` are added by the Phase 4 correction passes and governed by `al.candidate-a.rev2-candidate`; the Residential-34 profile references their residential subset without duplicating ownership. Deterministic resolution uses the full key `locale + normalizedText + platformDomainScope + identityKind`, so identical text in another platform domain is not a collision. Every active residential category has at least one EN and RU alias; exact display-label variants required by the punctuation-preserving preprocessing profile are included. Bedroom-specialization aliases resolve to their own `subtype.residential.*` identities, and kitchen_living_room aliases resolve to `space.residential.kitchen_living_room`, never to the composite metadata ID. No new KK alias is added; all pre-existing KK aliases remain unchanged.

# Annex V — Global Facility-to-Space Applicability Registry

This registry contains 1,066 exact same-domain Facility Context × Canonical Space Type rows (1,013 from Revision 18, plus 53 new residential rows added by Revision 19 — 48 for the 10 standalone new canonical types, plus 5 for `space.residential.kitchen_living_room`):

```text
Owner-selected Layer 2 applicability rows: 651 (598 from Revision 18, plus 53 new residential rows, all core/classification_target/visual_inference)
Layer 1-only structural applicability rows: 415 (unchanged)
Total rows: 1066
```

The first 651 rows (598 from Revision 18, plus the 53 added by Revision 19) reproduce the governing Owner-selected matrix semantics exactly. Their `evidenceMode` is `visual_inference`. The source directive contained a raw Markdown delimiter after that field; the stray presentation token is not imported as a second evidence mode. The remaining 415 rows extend only Layer 1 structural applicability and do not authorize classification, evaluation or activation.

# Annex Y — Shared Concept Group Registry

Exactly fifteen unique logical Shared Concept Group IDs are defined. Presentation formatting is non-semantic and is excluded from stable-ID comparison. The Owner-selected baseline policy is preserved for every group; Layer 1-only members are merged into the same successor row without creating a duplicate policy source.

# Annex Z — Cross-Domain Boundaries and Mandatory Discriminative Definitions

## Z.1 Cross-domain boundaries

| identityA | identityB | mandatoryBoundary |
|---|---|---|
| facility.beauty_personal_care.beauty_cosmetology_studio | facility.healthcare.medical_cosmetology_clinic | non-medical beauty cosmetology versus clinically regulated medical cosmetology |
| facility.beauty_personal_care.beauty_salon | facility.wellness_fitness.wellness_spa | beauty-service establishment versus wellness/spa establishment |
| space.beauty_personal_care.massage_treatment_room | space.healthcare.therapy_room | non-clinical beauty massage versus medical massage or clinical therapy |

## Z.2 Sanitary rooms

The four residential sanitary identities are mutually exclusive. A hygienic spray is not a bathing shower. When defining fixtures are not visible, the outcome is `insufficient_evidence_or_bounded_alternative_set`.

## Z.3 Residential circulation

`entryway`, `vestibule`, `hall`, `corridor`, `staircase_space`, and `stair_hall` retain distinct definitions in Annex S. `hallway` is an alias, not a Canonical Space Type. A stair landing is a Component.

## Z.4 Healthcare diagnostics

`diagnostic_room` is a separately bounded room primarily configured for non-imaging diagnostic testing using dedicated equipment; it excludes ordinary consultation, basic examination, minor procedure, and specialized imaging rooms.

## Z.5 Room versus zone

An enclosed room and an open Functional Zone with the same business purpose remain different identity kinds.

## AB.2 Platform Domain to DesignDomainId Binding Registry

This registry contains only actual current runtime `DesignDomainId` values. Gap records are owned separately by Annex AB.3.

| runtimeDesignDomainId | targetPlatformDomainIds | mappingCardinality | bindingStatus | reason |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| residential | platform.residential | one_to_one | mapped_exact | direct runtime domain mapping |
| commercial | platform.workplace<br>platform.retail<br>platform.wellness_fitness<br>platform.beauty_personal_care<br>platform.professional_services | one_to_many | mapped_via_legacy_umbrella | legacy commercial umbrella includes Professional Services but is not a dedicated exact domain |
| hospitality | platform.hospitality | one_to_one | mapped_exact | direct runtime domain mapping |
| public | platform.public_civic<br>platform.culture_entertainment<br>platform.religious_community | one_to_many | mapped_via_legacy_umbrella | legacy public umbrella |
| outdoor | null | not_applicable | axis_not_domain | represented by Space Environment, not Platform Domain |
| industrial | platform.service_light_industrial<br>platform.warehouse_logistics | one_to_many | mapped_via_legacy_umbrella | legacy industrial umbrella |
| entertainment | platform.culture_entertainment | one_to_one | mapped_exact | direct runtime domain mapping |
| transportation | platform.transport_travel | one_to_one | mapped_exact | direct runtime domain mapping |
| healthcare | platform.healthcare | one_to_one | mapped_exact | direct runtime domain mapping |
| education | platform.education | one_to_one | mapped_exact | direct runtime domain mapping |
| mixed_use | null | not_applicable | axis_not_domain | represented by Property Composition, not Platform Domain |

|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| rb.domain-gap.001 | platform.professional_services | reachable_only_via_commercial_legacy_umbrella | dedicated_extension_pending | the current runtime has no dedicated Professional Services value; the legacy `commercial` umbrella is not an exact one-to-one binding | not_authorized |

# Annex AD — Historical Drafting Lineage

Revisions 13–17 remain immutable historical drafting artifacts. Revision 18 does not edit them in place. Their governance outcomes and available hashes are retained in §1 and §1.1 for traceability; none is eligible for current candidate-lock issuance or downstream current-reference use.

Revision 18 itself became, in turn, an immutable historical drafting artifact once Revision 19 began: Revision 18 passed its full consolidated review (Candidate Lock C1-REV18-CL-001 issued, not repository-persisted) and is preserved without edit; Revision 19 imports it as the direct upstream baseline (§1.1) and does not alter any Revision 18 content except for the explicit additions and corrections recorded in Annex AF below.

# Annex AF — Revision 19 Targeted Extension Record

**Traceability reading rule.** Annex AF is chronological. Earlier drafting-step statements are historical intermediate states and are superseded by later AF subsections where they conflict. The current Revision 19 state is the state recorded by AF.5 together with the normative registries themselves; an earlier intermediate statement must never override a later correction.

```text
Current draft:
Contract 1 Revision 19

Immediate predecessor:
Contract 1 Revision 18 — full consolidated review PASS (BLOCKER 0,
MAJOR 0, MINOR 0, IMPROVEMENT 0); Candidate Lock C1-REV18-CL-001
issued, not repository-persisted;
SHA-256 8318333b47a4e98e1e4c7a098a243838fde2257f6a560f93445002399a8aae89

Candidate Lock:
C1-REV19-CL-001 — reserved, not issued (draft record prepared
alongside this document)

Final consolidated review of Revision 19:
NOT YET PERFORMED

Drafting authorization:
Project Owner instruction, Phase 4 of the accepted root-up
architecture correction Work Plan, 2026-07-22
```

Revision 19 additions and corrections completed in this drafting step, relative to Revision 18:

1. **11 new Layer 1 Canonical Space Type rows added to Annex S**, all immediately Owner-selected: `dining_room`, `home_office`, `library`, `pantry`, `laundry_room`, `utility_room`, `mechanical_room`, `basement`, `veranda`, `winter_garden`. The first drafting step added the 10 standalone rows; the later identity-model correction added `space.residential.kitchen_living_room`, producing the final counts 195→206 total and 132→143 Layer 2 Candidate-selected.
2. **4 new Space Families added to Annex R**: `family.residential.dining`, `family.residential.work_study`, `family.residential.utility`, `family.residential.below_grade`. Annex R header count updated 71→75. Family assignments for the 10 standalone additions are recorded in Annex S. The canonical `space.residential.kitchen_living_room` uses `family.residential.primary_living` as its single taxonomy parent; its food-preparation component is represented orthogonally by Annex AG, avoiding a multi-parent hierarchy and adding no new Space Family.
3. **3 new Space Subtype entries added to Annex S1** (previously a valid empty registry): `subtype.residential.children_room`, `subtype.residential.guest_bedroom`, `subtype.residential.primary_bedroom`, all with `parentCanonicalSpaceTypeId = space.residential.bedroom`.
4. **1 new Composite Space Profile added to new Annex AG**: `composite.residential.kitchen_living_room`, components `space.residential.living_room` + `space.residential.kitchen`. No Composite Space Profile mechanism existed anywhere in Revision 18 (verified by full-text search for "composite" — zero matches); Annex AG is a wholly new structural registry, not a new row in an existing table.
5. **Layer 2 / Profile Activation transition (Annex AA)**: 19 new activation entries were added (`L2-ACT-0286`–`L2-ACT-0304`). Of these, 15 are `active_candidate` (11 canonical room identities, 3 bedroom subtype identities and 1 auxiliary Composite Space Profile metadata record); the 4 supporting family identities are `inactive`. The 20 pre-existing residential canonical-space-type activation entries (`L2-ACT-0007`–`L2-ACT-0034`, excluding `carport`) were updated to `active_candidate`. `active_locked` is not assigned to any entry by this revision (reserved for explicit Project Owner acceptance of the complete Atomic Root-Transition Package — Bounded Scope + Contract 1 Revision 19 + Evaluation Threshold Plan successor + Test Data Handling successor + Module Applicability Profile successor; Annex J.3; Contract 2 revalidation and Contracts 3-10 drafting are separate Phase 5+ dependency activities, not part of this trigger). The 20 pre-existing residential canonical-space-type rows in Annex S were updated from `layer2SelectionStatus = owner_selected_not_yet_effective` to `owner_selected_effective`; the 11 new canonical rows were created directly at `owner_selected_effective`. Residential facility-context and supporting-family identities are not additional user-facing room categories and do not transition beyond their separately governed status.
6. **Annex J.3 / Annex AA status normalization**: an earlier intermediate drafting pass inherited the obsolete Annex AA literal `not_yet_effective`. The final Revision 19 registry uses the authoritative closed enum `inactive | active_candidate | active_locked | profile_deprecated`; no current Annex AA row uses `not_yet_effective`. The 34 room categories and one auxiliary Composite Space Profile metadata record are `active_candidate`; the four newly added supporting families are `inactive`; `active_locked` is assigned nowhere.
7. **Facility-to-Space applicability (Annex V)**: 53 new rows total — 48 rows for the 10 standalone canonical additions (8 × 5 facility contexts, plus `mechanical_room` × 4 and `basement` × 4, both omitting `apartment`) and 5 direct rows for `space.residential.kitchen_living_room`.
8. **Localization**: 15 new EN rows added to Annex U1 (`loc.full.0272`–`loc.full.0286`): 11 canonical room identities, 3 new subtypes, and 1 separate Composite Space Profile metadata label. 14 new EN+RU row-pairs added to Annex A0.3 (Active Space Localization Registry) for the same 14 identities, following A0.3's existing `profile.space.*` / EN / RU / KK / `sp.candidate-a.five-room.1.0.0-candidate` row format, with KK label and KK localization status represented separately: existing labels preserved and missing labels marked `not_yet_localized` without placing a status token in the label field, per the governing Phase 4 instruction's explicit "do not expand KK" boundary. **Historical first-pass state, subsequently remediated in AF.2**: Annex A0.3 initially carried only 5 of the 20 pre-existing residential canonical types (`living_room`, `bedroom`, `kitchen`, `bathroom`, `toilet_room`) before this revision; the other 15 pre-existing residential canonical types (`shower_room`, `combined_bathroom`, `entryway`, `vestibule`, `hall`, `corridor`, `dressing_room`, `walk_in_closet`, `staircase_space`, `stair_hall`, `attic`, `mansard_room`, `garage`, `balcony`, `terrace`) have no Annex A0.3 row at all, in Revision 18 or in this Revision 19 — this gap predated the first Revision 19 pass and was later closed in AF.2; it is not a current condition of the final artifact.
9. **Annex AB.1 legacy runtime alignment**: `dining_room` and
`home_office` changed from unresolved legacy values to
`canonical_binding_ready` because the repository snapshot verifies exact
residential runtime literals. Generic legacy literals `library` and
`terrace` remain `unresolved_legacy_runtime_value` / `no_exact_target`:
their existence in the generic runtime inventory does not verify an exact
Residential-34 semantic binding, and cross-domain guessing is prohibited.
Annex A0.2 therefore keeps both residential categories at
`registry_extension_required` with runtime emission unauthorized. Other
unresolved legacy values remain unchanged unless an exact governed target
is separately verified.
10. **New Annex AH — Track E Compatibility Identity Hooks**: final registry contains 17 governed rows: 3 current bounded-operation identities (`OperationId`, `RoomCaseId`, `ImageAssetId`) and 14 reserved future-compatibility hooks, added as a new structural registry, consistent with the already-persisted Track E Compatibility Foundation language in Full-Platform Vision Architecture Revision 5 §11.1 (in-place corrected, commit `f57b3f8cf34757997e6d1e2f23e67629083687f2`) and Candidate A Bounded Scope Decision Revision 5 §8E (in-place corrected, commit `565a3a03294086f319ccec5ff2e77afb5af8a9e1`).
11. Sections 2, 2a, 8, 9, 10, 11, and 12 were updated to reference the 34-category scope, the Composite Space Profile, and the Track E compatibility hooks; none of their pre-existing Revision 18 content was removed or reversed.
12. No other vocabulary definition, stable ID, applicability decision, activation entry, runtime binding, schema meaning or authorization boundary from Revision 18 was changed. In particular: all 16 Platform Domains; all 195 pre-existing Layer 1 Canonical Space Types; the sanitary four-category model (Annex Z §Z.2, already resolved in Revision 18); Mechanism Class B compatibility vocabulary; and the complete non-authorization boundary (Section 12, as extended, not narrowed) remain exactly as Revision 18 defined them.

This record is a drafting trace, not an architectural-review PASS and not a Candidate Lock issuance record. Final consolidated review of Revision 19 is a separate, later action against the exact artifact this record describes.

### AF.1 Phase 4 Final Correction Pass (second drafting step, same Revision 19)

The findings below were identified in an architectural review of the first Revision 19 draft (SHA-256 `9c2cd464b3797481b01e5238d6132a730f3f16ce5e366df7156297de3bf6084f`, 4885 lines) and are corrected in this same file, in place — no Revision 20 is created.

```text
1.  §5.1 Rule 2 ("Active Evaluation Profile remains five-room bounded")
    — removed; replaced with the 34-category normative statement.
    The historical "five-room" token now survives only inside
    explicitly labelled immutable historical version identifiers.
2.  §4.2 closed enums corrected: Layer2SelectionStatus gained
    owner_selected_effective; effectiveActivationStatus reconciled
    to the same closed enum as profileActivationStatus (no duplicate
    axis). All 265 non-residential Annex AA rows bulk-corrected from
    the no-longer-valid literal not_yet_effective to inactive.
3.  kitchen_living_room identity model corrected: a new canonical
    Annex S row, space.residential.kitchen_living_room, is now THE
    stable Room.spaceType/CanonicalSpaceTypeId-facing identity.
    composite.residential.kitchen_living_room (Annex AG) is
    explicitly reduced to auxiliary composite metadata only. This
    changes the addition count: 206 Canonical Space Types (not 205),
    143 Layer 2-selected (not 142), 304 Layer 2 Activation entries
    (not 303) — the "10 canonical + 3 specialization + 1 composite =
    14 total additions" count (Section 6 of the governing correction
    instruction) is preserved at the level of 14 distinct user-facing
    identities; kitchen_living_room, as the 14th, is realized through
    two registry touchpoints (an Annex S canonical row and an Annex
    AG metadata row), exactly as each of the 3 specializations is
    realized through two touchpoints (an Annex S1 row and an Annex AA
    row).
4.  Annex A0.2 (SpaceProfileBinding Registry) — 14 new binding rows
    added (c1.space.006-019), covering all 10 standalone new
    canonical types, kitchen_living_room, and the 3 subtypes, each
    with its own distinct exported downstream identity, so that none
    of children_room/guest_bedroom/primary_bedroom/kitchen_living_room
    collapses into bedroom/living_room/kitchen. A new version token,
    sp.candidate-a.residential-34.1.0.0-candidate, distinguishes these
    rows from the 5 historical five-room rows.
5.  Applicability closure added for the 3 subtypes (Annex S1, new
    §S1.1: explicit inherited applicability from space.residential.bedroom,
    with precedence, exceptions, ownership and evidence-inheritance
    stated) and for the composite (Annex AG, new §AG.1: explicit
    statement that Annex V is directly covered, Annex T/U/W/X inherit
    the same "none" pattern as the two components).
6.  A single, explicit localization ownership rule added (Annex A0.3
    intro): Annex U1 owns EN completely; Annex A0.3 owns RU (and
    historical KK) for the active profile, incompletely, with the
    pre-existing 15-row gap explicitly named as not remediated. This
    was an historical intermediate drafting rule; it is superseded by
    AF.5 and the current Annex A0.3 rule requiring explicit, traceable
    canonical-EN fallback when a requested non-EN locale unit is
    unavailable.
7.  Registry versions bumped for every registry whose content Revision
    19 actually changed: canonicalSpaceType (Annex S)
    mv.space.candidate-a.3.0.0→4.0.0-candidate; layer2Candidate (Annex
    AA) l2.candidate-a.rev1→rev2-candidate, and its 304 row-level
    profileVersion cells L2-PROFILE-REV1→REV2-CANDIDATE; applicability
    (Annexes V-X) app.candidate-a.rev1→rev2-candidate; aliasLocalization
    (Annexes U1-U2) al.candidate-a.rev1→rev2-candidate; runtimeBinding
    (Annex AB) rb.candidate-a.rev1→rev2-candidate; record schema (Annex
    L) rs.candidate-a.3.0.0→4.0.0-candidate; a new residentialRoomProfile
    version, sp.candidate-a.residential-34.1.0.0-candidate, introduced
    for the new Annex A0.2/A0.3 rows only. The historical
    vp.candidate-a.five-room.1.0.0-candidate (Annex B, unchanged
    content) is explicitly NOT bumped, consistent with item 1's rule
    that unedited historical registries keep their historical version.
8.  Annex K (Candidate-Lock Readiness Snapshot) — now states the
    current Revision 19 snapshot (C1-REV19-CL-001); the Revision 18
    snapshot is retained as historical §K.1, explicitly marked as not
    describing Revision 19. Annex L — record schema registry
    corrected: version bumped, CompositeSpaceProfileEntry and
    TrackECompatibilityHookEntry added, all counts synchronized. §7 is
    unchanged in this Contract (it already read "Revision 18
    validation" only in the historical sense of describing what
    Revision 18's own validation pass covered; Revision 19's own
    validation is this correction pass itself, recorded here in Annex
    AF, not by editing §7's historical description).
9.  Annex V counts corrected throughout (introduction, §8.2, this
    Annex, RegistryVersionSet, Annex L): 1,066 total rows (not 1,061)
    — 1,013 from Revision 18 plus 53 new residential rows (48 for the
    10 standalone canonical types, plus 5 for
    space.residential.kitchen_living_room), not 48 alone.
10. Annex R — explicit confirmation added that the 4 new families
    follow the same owner_selected_not_yet_effective status as every

    room categories, and do not expand the 34-category activation

12. New Annex AI (Cross-Cutting Compatibility Foundations) added:

    vp.candidate-a.five-room.1.0.0-candidate (Annex B, object profile)

14. Final consistency pass performed: exact count 206/143/304/1066/19

    confirmed as one of the 34, not a 35th; balanced Markdown code
```

This second drafting step does not itself constitute a new architectural review PASS. It records only that the findings from the prior review have been closed in this same Revision 19 file.

```text

    confirmation. KK unchanged (5 pre-existing translations

    without fabrication). The earlier no-fallback wording was an

    canonical-EN fallback rule.
```

# Annex AG — Composite Space Profile Registry

New structural registry, added by Revision 19. No Composite Space Profile mechanism existed in Revision 18 — verified by a full-text search of the complete Revision 18 artifact for the token "composite," which returned zero matches. This registry is the sole normative owner of every Composite Space Profile **metadata** identity, exactly as Annex S is the sole normative owner of every canonical Space Type and Annex S1 the sole normative owner of every Space Subtype.

**Identity model (corrected per the Phase 4 final correction pass):**

The canonical room type uses `family.residential.primary_living` as its single taxonomy parent. The kitchen component is represented by this Annex AG metadata record, so the hierarchy remains parent-complete and single-parent while the composite evidence model remains explicit.

```text
space.residential.kitchen_living_room (Annex S, canonical_space_type):
  THE stable, user-facing room-type identity. Room.spaceType and every
  CanonicalSpaceTypeId reference use this identity directly. It is a
  normal canonical Space Type row like any of the other 33 residential
  categories, with its own full normativeDefinition, evidence,
  applicability, activation and localization records throughout this
  Contract, counted as one of the 34.

composite.residential.kitchen_living_room (this Annex AG,
  composite_space_profile):
  AUXILIARY metadata about that one canonical identity, capturing the
  composition-specific facts (component identities, evidence
  methodology, dominance/precedence rules) that do not fit the
  standard Annex S record schema. It is never itself a Room.spaceType
  value, never a CanonicalSpaceTypeId reference target, and never
  counted as a distinct, additional room category — it describes
  space.residential.kitchen_living_room, it does not compete with it.
```

A Composite Space Profile represents one physical room that simultaneously exhibits the defining positive evidence of two or more component Canonical Space Types, within one continuous, non-partitioned space. It is never a multi-room construct and never implies more than one `RoomCase` (Candidate A Bounded Scope Decision Rev5, Section 8A, in-place corrected).

| compositeProfileId | describesCanonicalSpaceTypeId | componentCanonicalSpaceTypeIds | compositionSemantics | evidenceMethodology | dominanceRule | precedenceRule | inheritanceRule | structuredSceneCompatibility | layer2SelectionStatus | lifecycleStatus |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| composite.residential.kitchen_living_room | space.residential.kitchen_living_room | space.residential.living_room<br>space.residential.kitchen | One continuous, non-partitioned physical room exhibiting the defining positive evidence of both `living_room` and `kitchen` simultaneously. | Independently sufficient evidence is required for both component functions (living-room seating/focal-point evidence and kitchen cooking/food-preparation evidence), not necessarily within the same `ImageAsset` of the governed 1–6 capture set. The exact numeric evidentiary threshold is not decided by this Contract — reserved for the Evaluation Threshold Plan / Contract 6 (Determinability) / Contract 7 (Semantic Case, Scenario, Sufficiency and Completeness) corpus calibration. | If evidence is sufficient for only one component, classification falls back to that single component's own canonical identity (`living_room` or `kitchen`), not to `space.residential.kitchen_living_room` — this is a partial-evidence fallback, not an ambiguity failure. If evidence is insufficient for either component individually but the space is confirmed continuous and non-partitioned, the case is `InsufficientEvidenceResult`, not a forced classification. | A full separating wall and door between the two functional zones places them as two separate rooms, classified individually as `living_room` and `kitchen` (two operations, not one composite classification) — `space.residential.kitchen_living_room` applies only to one continuous, non-partitioned space. | Inherits the union (not the intersection) of both components' relation and object/material applicability once Contract 3 defines it; not decided by this Contract. | Represented through the existing `Room.spaceType` reference mechanism (ADR-013 §4.2) as a direct reference to `space.residential.kitchen_living_room` (never to `composite.residential.kitchen_living_room`) — exactly as any other `SpaceTypeId` reference. No new node, relation, or attribute category is added to `StructuredScene v0`, and no change to ADR-013 is required (consistent with the separately performed ADR-013 revalidation, Root Impact Analysis Phase 3, conclusion: content change not required). | owner_selected_effective | active |

Future extension of this registry to additional Composite Space Profiles (in any domain) follows the same schema; this revision adds exactly one row, describing exactly one canonical Space Type.

### AG.1 Applicability closure (Phase 4 final correction pass)

```text
Annex V (Facility-to-Space Applicability):
space.residential.kitchen_living_room has its own five direct rows
(one per residential Facility Context), added by Revision 19. No
inheritance is required for Annex V.

Annex T/U/W/X (Functional Zone / Space Use / their applicability
registries):
space.residential.kitchen_living_room carries none/not_applicable in
its own Annex S row, exactly as its two components
(space.residential.living_room, space.residential.kitchen) already
do. No row is required, inherited, or omitted — this is the same
"none" pattern already used for the great majority of residential
canonical types, not a gap specific to the composite case.

Relation/object/material applicability (future Contract 3):
inherits the union of both components' applicability once Contract 3
defines it (already stated in the registry row above); not decided
by this Contract.
```

# Annex AH — Track E Compatibility Identity Hooks

New structural registry, added by Revision 19. Reserved, additive, non-activating identity namespace, consistent with the Track E Compatibility Foundation already made explicit in Full-Platform Vision Architecture Revision 5 §11.1 (in-place corrected) and Candidate A Bounded Scope Decision Revision 5 §8A/§8E (in-place corrected). None of the identities below is populated with real data, bound to a runtime schema, or activated by this Contract.

| hookIdentityId | describedFutureRole | currentActivationStatus | boundToCurrentIdentity | activatesOnAuthority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| hook.trackE.PropertyId | Future identity for a physical property, above the current Operation scope. | reserved_not_activated | none | separate future Track E opening decision |
| hook.trackE.ProjectId | Future identity for a design project, above the current Operation scope. | reserved_not_activated | none | separate future Track E opening decision |
| hook.trackE.SessionId | Future identity for a user session spanning multiple operations. | reserved_not_activated | none | separate future Track E opening decision |
| hook.trackE.OperationId | Current, already-active identity for one bounded perception operation. | active_now | Candidate A Bounded Scope Decision Rev5, Section 8A | already authorized — current bounded operation |
| hook.trackE.RoomCaseId | Current, already-active identity for one physical room within one operation (`Operation.RoomCase` = exactly 1 now; `RoomCase[1..N]` architectural capacity reserved). | active_now | Candidate A Bounded Scope Decision Rev5, Section 8A | already authorized — current bounded operation |
| hook.trackE.ImageAssetId | Current, already-active identity for one governed image within a 1–6 capture set. | active_now | Candidate A Bounded Scope Decision Rev5, Section 8A/8A.1 | already authorized — current bounded operation |
| hook.trackE.future_RoomViewId | Future identity for a persistent, cross-operation view of one room. | reserved_not_activated | optional future `RoomCase → RoomView` binding (Bounded Scope Rev5, Section 8A) | separate future Track E opening decision |
| hook.trackE.future_RoomStateVersionId | Future identity for a versioned snapshot of one room's state over time. | reserved_not_activated | none | separate future Track E opening decision |
| hook.trackE.future_InterRoomRelationRef | Future reference for a relation between two distinct `RoomView` instances (e.g. adjacency, shared wall) once multi-room execution is opened. | reserved_not_activated | none | separate future Track E opening decision |
| hook.trackE.future_ProjectMemoryRef | Future reference into a persistent, cross-session Project Memory store. | reserved_not_activated | none | separate future Track E opening decision |
| hook.trackE.future_CrossSessionProvenanceRef | Future reference linking evidence/provenance (Perception Mechanism Architecture Rev3, Part M, as corrected) across separate sessions, rather than within one bounded operation only. | reserved_not_activated | none | separate future Track E opening decision |
| hook.trackE.future_CameraPoseRef | Future reference for camera position/orientation associated with one `ImageAsset`, for 2.5D/3D reconstruction. | reserved_not_activated | none | separate future Track E opening decision |
| hook.trackE.future_DepthRef | Future reference for per-pixel or per-region depth data associated with one `ImageAsset`. | reserved_not_activated | none | separate future Track E opening decision |
| hook.trackE.future_ScaleRef | Future reference for a real-world scale/metric calibration of one `RoomView`. | reserved_not_activated | none | separate future Track E opening decision |
| hook.trackE.future_CoordinateSystemRef | Future reference for the shared coordinate system a `RoomView` or multi-room graph is expressed in. | reserved_not_activated | none | separate future Track E opening decision |
| hook.trackE.future_FloorPlanAlignmentRef | Future reference aligning one or more `RoomView` instances to a floor-plan document. | reserved_not_activated | none | separate future Track E opening decision |
| hook.trackE.future_SourceModalityRef | Future reference identifying the capture modality of a source (e.g. photo, LiDAR scan, video frame) beyond the current bounded photo-only capture set. | reserved_not_activated | none | separate future Track E opening decision |

`currentActivationStatus` is a closed value: `reserved_not_activated | active_now`. No third value is introduced. Every `reserved_not_activated` row above is additive and non-activating; it creates no implementation, corpus, evaluation, or runtime duty (consistent with §5.1 Rule 19).

None of the following is activated by this registry or by this Contract: persistent `RoomView`; cross-session memory; multi-room execution; whole-home topology; Project Memory; video; panorama; 2.5D/3D or full 3D reconstruction; LiDAR or other non-photo capture modalities (Bounded Scope Rev5, Section 8E, in-place corrected).

# Annex AI — Cross-Cutting Compatibility Foundations (Contract-1-Owned Hooks)

New structural registry, added by Revision 19 (Phase 4 final correction pass; versioned as a governed registry, and completed with concrete per-foundation hooks, by the Phase 4 final consolidated closure correction pass). This registry adds only the minimal Contract-1-owned vocabulary, identity, namespace, version-reference, provenance-link, eligibility-reference, and rollback/no-regression compatibility hooks required for compatibility with five cross-cutting foundations already established elsewhere (principally Full-Platform Vision Architecture Rev5 §15.2/§15.3/§15.4/§16.2, in-place corrected). It does not open, own, or duplicate any of the five foundations as a full architecture — each remains owned by its own governing document. No foundation is implemented, activated, or made `active_locked` by this registry.

Registry version: `cc.candidate-a.1.0.0-candidate`. Because Revision 19 has not been accepted or locked, this same candidate version token remains the final Rev19 candidate registry version throughout this drafting step; no mechanically necessary successor token is triggered within this still-unlocked draft.

| compatibilityHookId | foundationId | ownedByThisContract | describedHook | specificHooksReferenced | provenanceReference | activationStatus | introducedVersion |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| hook.crossCutting.colorMaterial | foundation.colorMaterial | Contract 1 reserved vocabulary/identity references only | Annex J.6 reserves `material.*`, `finish.*`, `color.*`, and `color_space.*` namespaces as non-populated compatibility hooks for a future separately authorized Color & Material foundation. No concrete material or color identity is claimed to exist in Annex G by this hook. | CIELAB governed reference kind; CIEXYZ governed reference kind; CIEDE2000 comparison-method reference; sRGB reference; HEX reference; ICC profile reference; reserved material identity reference (`material.*`); reserved finish identity reference (`finish.*`); reserved color identity reference (`color.*`); reserved color-space/profile identity reference (`color_space.*`); source/profile version reference. **Pantone is explicitly not required, not planned, and not a dependency of this Contract or this hook.** | Annex J.6 reserved namespaces; Full-Platform Vision Architecture Rev5 Color & Material compatibility boundary | compatibility_only | cc.candidate-a.1.0.0-candidate |
| hook.crossCutting.diagnosability | foundation.diagnosability | Contract 1 vocabulary/identity only | Every Layer 1/Layer 2 identity in this Contract (canonical, subtype, composite, family, facility context, Track E hook) carries a stable, unique ID (Section 5.1, Rule 3) usable as a diagnostic trace anchor. **Explicit separation rule: semantic evidence explains semantic claims; processing diagnostics explain processing behavior and failures. They are related by trace references but are not the same artifact.** This hook does not contradict, narrow, or reassign Annex I: Contract 1 already owns the Annex I FailureCode Registry and `NormalizationRecord.failureCode`; this hook reserves additional future diagnostic namespaces without denying those existing Contract-1 failure-code definitions. | semantic trace identity; diagnostic code namespace (additive to Annex I, not replacing it); processing diagnostic reference; semantic evidence reference; result reference; evidence-to-result linkage; diagnostic-to-operation linkage. | Annex I (FailureCode Registry, unchanged); Perception Mechanism Architecture Rev3, Part M; Full-Platform Vision Architecture Rev5 §15.2 | compatibility_only | cc.candidate-a.1.0.0-candidate |
| hook.crossCutting.securityPrivacy | foundation.securityPrivacy | Contract 1 vocabulary/identity only | Contract 1 identities carry no personal or user data; Track E hooks (Annex AH) are explicitly reserved and non-activating, so no new security/privacy surface is introduced by this revision. Full Security & Privacy Foundation ownership remains with Full-Platform Vision Architecture Rev5 §15.3 (as corrected) and the separately gated future Security Architecture cycle. **No personal-data processing or new data collection is authorized by this hook.** | source eligibility reference; sensitivity classification reference; consent/data-use eligibility reference; retention-policy reference; security-policy version reference; privacy-policy version reference; access-control classification reference. | Full-Platform Vision Architecture Rev5 §15.3 | compatibility_only | cc.candidate-a.1.0.0-candidate |
| hook.crossCutting.enRuLocalization | foundation.enRuLocalization | Contract 1 vocabulary/identity only | EN canonical identity (Annex S, Annex S1, Annex AG, Annex U1) and RU user-facing localization (Annex A0.3) are provided for all 34 active residential categories (34/34 EN, 34/34 RU). Existing KK translations (5 pre-existing) are preserved and no new KK translation is invented. When a requested non-EN locale unit is unavailable, canonical EN is the mandatory fallback required by Full-Platform Vision Architecture Rev5 §15.4; fallback must be explicit and traceable, never silent. Contract 1 does not own the UI switch itself. | canonical EN reference; required RU reference; locale registry version reference; global locale switch compatibility reference; mandatory canonical-EN fallback policy; requestedLocale/resolvedLocale/fallbackApplied trace references; explicit `not_yet_localized` status. | Full-Platform Vision Architecture Rev5 §15.4 | compatibility_only | cc.candidate-a.1.0.0-candidate |
| hook.crossCutting.controlledLearning | foundation.controlledLearning | Contract 1 vocabulary/identity/versioning only | Every registry touched by this revision carries an explicit version token, and every new identity carries stable, immutable identifiers — both are prerequisites for the Minimal Controlled Learning Compatibility Foundation's hooks. **User feedback must never directly change production behavior.** No feedback collection, no analytics activation, no training, no automatic rule/model update, no rollout, and no automatic production-behavior change is authorized by this Contract. | model version reference; rule version reference; contract version reference; provenance/evidence linkage; future feedback-link identity (reserved, not activated); consent/data-use eligibility reference; immutable history reference; no-regression evaluation reference; rollback reference; deployment/rollout version reference (reserved, not activated). | Full-Platform Vision Architecture Rev5 §16.2 | compatibility_only | cc.candidate-a.1.0.0-candidate |

`activationStatus` is a closed value: `compatibility_only`. No other value is defined by this registry; none of the five foundations is opened, activated, or implemented by this Contract; none is or may become `active_locked`. All required hooks listed above are represented either as direct references or as governed reference fields; no undefined reference type is introduced.
